EDCS-02700358.translation: To Asteria, my sweetest and incomparable daughter, who lived for 5 years and 3 months.
EDCS-02700358.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Asteria, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-02900220.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Cosconius Potens made this for his dearest wife, Ferrania Tacita, who lived 25 years. [He] made [it].
EDCS-02900220.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ferrania Tacita, set up by her husband Lucius Cosconius Potens, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-02900228.translation: Lucius Coelius, son of Caius, of the Sabatina tribe, and Caellia, daughter of Lucius, of the same tribe, saw to the construction of the portico and schools from their own money.
EDCS-02900228.summary: An inscription recording the funding of a portico and schools by Lucius Coelius and Caellia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-02900233.translation: To our Lord Magnentius, the invincible, victorious, triumphant prince, forever Augustus, liberator of the Roman world, restorer of liberty and the Republic, and protector of provincial soldiers.
EDCS-02900233.summary: A dedication to the emperor Magnentius, likely set up by military officials, dating to the period of his reign (350-353 AD).

EDCS-03300200.summary: A fragmentary inscription mentioning Lucius Vesonius and the office of IIIIvir, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300201.translation: While alive: Gaius Persius, son of Aulus, of the Galeria tribe; while alive: Gallonia Quarta(?), daughter of Marcus; Lucius Persius, son of Gaius, son of Gaius; Persia Polla, daughter of Gaius.
EDCS-03300201.summary: A family inscription recording several individuals and their tribal affiliations, likely from the Roman Imperial period, with at least one person alive at the time of inscription.

EDCS-03300203.translation: Titus Manlius Apollodorus, freedman of Titus.
EDCS-03300203.summary: A funerary epitaph or dedication identifying Titus Manlius Apollodorus, a freedman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300206.translation: To Hercules [---]. Lucius Sertorius [---] and Pyrallis [---].
EDCS-03300206.summary: A fragmentary dedication to the god Hercules, mentioning Lucius Sertorius and Pyrallis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700207.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Daphne, sweet soul, lived 10 years [---] months.
EDCS-03700207.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Daphne, indicating her age at death, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700212.translation: Quintus Pedanius Felix and Pedania Iuvenca rebuilt the temple of Aera(cura) according to their vow.
EDCS-03700212.summary: An inscription recording the rebuilding of a temple dedicated to Aera(cura) by Quintus Pedanius Felix and Pedania Iuvenca, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700213.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo M(aximo) Lucius Resinna Marcellinus V\n
EDCS-03700213.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Resinna Marcellinus dedicated [this].
EDCS-03700213.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by Lucius Resinna Marcellinus.

EDCS-03700215.translation: Lucius Roscius, son of Marcus, of the Arnensis tribe.
EDCS-03700215.summary: This inscription is a name and tribal affiliation, likely from a funerary or ownership marker.

EDCS-03700228.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to be a dedication or a funerary notice for Marcus Aemilius.

EDCS-03700230.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely recording the name of Ruius, possibly in a dedicatory or funerary context.

EDCS-03700237.translation: ...of the college, after the lustrum, Asclepiades, servant of Aphrodisius...
EDCS-03700237.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely refers to a member of a college or association, possibly related to a religious or civic purification ritual.

EDCS-03700240.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Arria Asellia Sitara lived 65 years... [someone] made [this].
EDCS-03700240.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Arria Asellia Sitara, recording her age and that the monument was erected by someone.

EDCS-03700252.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Zosimus, son...
EDCS-03700252.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Zosimus.

EDCS-04200804.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Veturia Celerina, who died at the age of 15 years and 4 months. Titus Veturisus and Volgia Priscilla [made this].
EDCS-04200804.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Veturia Celerina, erected by her parents Titus Veturisus and Volgia Priscilla.

EDCS-04900269.translation: Caius Procilius, son of Caius; Aulus Procilius, son of Caius, of the Stellatina tribe. In front 15 feet [of land].
EDCS-04900269.summary: This inscription records land division or ownership, specifying front dimensions and identifying the owners with their tribal affiliations.

EDCS-04900271.translation: To holy Soranus Apollo, Tiberius Caius Atimetus gave this gift.
EDCS-04900271.summary: This is a dedication to the god Apollo Soranus by Tiberius Caius Atimetus.

EDCS-04900272.translation: To Calliope, the most excellent mother, Flavia Marcia, daughter, made this.
EDCS-04900272.summary: A dedication by Flavia Marcia to her mother, Calliope.

EDCS-04900274.translation: Curiatius Polio, son of Marcus, of the Voltinian tribe.
EDCS-04900274.summary: A funerary or identification marker for Curiatius Polio, son of Marcus.

EDCS-04900275.translation: Gaius Pescennius, son of Marcus, of the Voltinian tribe.
EDCS-04900275.summary: A funerary or identification marker for Gaius Pescennius, son of Marcus.

EDCS-04900276.translation: To Jupiter and Hercules, Publius Scribonius Proculus, a centurion of the cohort of the Caeretan water carriers, VI, fulfilled his vow for his merits.
EDCS-04900276.summary: A votive inscription by Publius Scribonius Proculus, a centurion in the fire brigade, to Jupiter and Hercules.

EDCS-05000214.translation: To the divine spirits, for Octavia Florilla, freedwoman of Gnaeus, by the arbitration of Lucius Octavius Avitianus, son of Publius.
EDCS-05000214.summary: A funerary dedication to Octavia Florilla, arranged by Lucius Octavius Avitianus.

EDCS-05000216.translation: Lucius Cornelius Macer, son of Lucius, of the Fabian tribe, of the 20th legion; Lucius Cornelius Primus, son of Lucius, son.
EDCS-05000216.summary: An inscription likely related to military service, mentioning two individuals named Lucius Cornelius.

EDCS-05000220.summary: A very brief inscription possibly referring to a grandfather named Bassus.

EDCS-05000221.summary: A brief inscription identifying Priscilla as a sister.

EDCS-05000222.summary: A brief inscription identifying Valerianus as a brother.

EDCS-05000230.translation: The fellow citizens, in honor of the benefits of Umbricius Alco, her father, for Umbricia Alce, daughter of Gaius.
EDCS-05000230.summary: An honorary inscription by fellow citizens for Umbricia Alce, honoring the benefits provided by her father, Umbricius Alco.

EDCS-05000234.translation: Gaius Ter... freedman of the CCaiorum...
EDCS-05000234.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary epitaph, for a freedman whose name and affiliation are partially preserved.

EDCS-05000237.translation: To the innocent daughter of my lady, Victorina, who lived for one year, five months, and fifteen days, (she) was returned on the day before the Nones of January, at the fourth hour of the day. In peace.
EDCS-05000237.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Victorina, recording her date of death and lifespan.

EDCS-05000240.translation: Aescionia, freedwoman of a woman, Hilar[a].
EDCS-05000240.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary epitaph, for a freedwoman named Hilar[a].

EDCS-05000241.translation: Gaius Valerius Faustus, freedman of Gaius, cattle merchant, chief Augustalis of Veis.
EDCS-05000241.summary: This is an inscription honoring Gaius Valerius Faustus, a prominent freedman and cattle merchant who held a leadership position in Veis.

EDCS-05000242.translation: Gaius Valerius Hilario, freedman of Gaius.
EDCS-05000242.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary epitaph, for a freedman named Hilario.

EDCS-05200199.translation: Lucius Voltinius Licinus, freedman of Lucius.
EDCS-05200199.summary: This is an inscription, likely a funerary epitaph, for Lucius Voltinius Licinus, a freedman.

EDCS-05200200.summary: This is a dedication recording a vow made by Numerius Pullius.

EDCS-05200207.text_edition: \n Caius Vettienus Nedymus et Vettiena Libas fecerunt sibi et Lanthanusae filiae vixit annos XXII\n
EDCS-05200207.translation: Caius Vettienus Nedymus and Vettiena Libas made this for themselves and for their daughter Lanthanusa, who lived 22 years.
EDCS-05200207.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lanthanusa, erected by her parents, Caius Vettienus Nedymus and Vettiena Libas.

EDCS-05200208.text_edition: \n aedilis curulis et honore usus duumvir iure dicundo duumvir quinquennalis pontifex curator pecuniae publicae iterum a decurionibus per tabellam creatus testamento fieri iussit arbitratu Campatiae Spuri filiae Rufillae uxoris\n
EDCS-05200208.translation: He held the office of curule aedile and used the honor of duovir with the right to pronounce judgment, duovir for five years, pontifex, curator of public funds, chosen for the second time by the decurions by vote; he ordered it to be made by will under the supervision of his wife Campatia Rufilla, daughter of Spurius.
EDCS-05200208.summary: This inscription details the extensive public career and last will of an unnamed magistrate, who appointed his wife as executor of his burial arrangements.

EDCS-05200209.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Constantino Maximo Pio Felici Augusto bono rei publicae nato votis X votis XX XXXV\n
EDCS-05200209.translation: To our lord Constantine Maximus Pius Felix Augustus, born for the good of the republic, for the vows of 10, for the vows of 20, for 35 years.
EDCS-05200209.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Constantine, likely commemorating imperial acclamations or anniversaries of his reign.

EDCS-05200210.text_edition: \n dddominis nnnostris Caio Valerio Diocletiano et Caio Aurelio Valerio Maximiano et Caio Valerio Constantio et Caio Valerio Galerio Maximiano nobilissimis Caessaribus milia passuum XXXIII\n
EDCS-05200210.translation: To our lords Diocletian, Maximian, Constantius, and Galerius Maximian, most noble Caesars, 33 miles.
EDCS-05200210.summary: This is a milestone recording a distance of 33 miles dedicated to the emperors Diocletian, Maximian, Constantius, and Galerius Maximian.

EDCS-05200211.text_edition: \n Titus Plotius Alexander Flavia Publi liberta Eris Flavia |(mulieris) liberta Agatia liebertis sic libertabus posterisque eorum\n
EDCS-05200211.translation: Titus Plotius Alexander and Eris, freedwoman of Flavia Publi and freedwoman of Agatia, to their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-05200211.summary: This inscription records a grant of freedom and rights to freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants, likely by Titus Plotius Alexander and Eris.

EDCS-05200214.text_edition: \n ------] Titinia Pii Metilia Tit Pompeia Q C Pompeia Quinta Pompeia Prisca ebia abi Pat \n
EDCS-05200214.translation: [---] Titinia Pii, Metilia Tit, and Pompeia Quinta, Pompeia Prisca, [---] daughter of Ebius [---].
EDCS-05200214.summary: This is a fragmented inscription that likely lists names of women, possibly in a familial or legal context, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05200216.text_edition: \n ------] ar a Potita tia Polla Sabina Quarta Salvia Taciti Cassia Cassi in cohorte Tra aesia Prisca tronia Veri et Palli ria Fructi toria Medi \n
EDCS-05200216.translation: [---] from Potita,atia Polla, Sabina Quarta, Salvia Taciti, Cassia Cassi, in the cohort [---] Traesia Prisca, Tron ia Veri, and Palli ria Fructi toria Medi [---].
EDCS-05200216.summary: This is a fragmented inscription that appears to list names of women, possibly related to military service or a local association, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05400987.text_edition: \n Clarus Petiniae Posillae vilicus compitialis aram Laris suscepto voto laetus sacravit libens merito\n
EDCS-05400987.translation: Clarus, supervisor of the neighborhood shrine, gladly and deservedly dedicated this altar to Larus, fulfilling a vow.
EDCS-05400987.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar by Clarus, a vilicus compitialis, to the Lares, likely in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-06100231.text_edition: \n Iovi Fonti Aquarum Caeretanarum Florentinus Augusti dono dedit\n
EDCS-06100231.translation: Florentinus, a servant of Augustus, gave this to Iovis Fonti Aquarum Caeretanarum.
EDCS-06100231.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a gift by Florentinus, a servant of Augustus, to the Iovis Fonti Aquarum Caeretanarum.

EDCS-06100233.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Diodoto Tauri filio medico Tyanensi ex Cappadocia Charinus alumnus bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-06100233.translation: To the divine spirits: Charinus, his pupil, made this for Diodotus, son of Taurus, a physician from Tyanensis in Cappadocia, who deserved well.
EDCS-06100233.summary: This is a tombstone inscription dedicated by Charinus to his well-deserving teacher, Diodotus, a physician from Cappadocia.

EDCS-06100235.text_edition: \n Lucius Decumius Luci libertus Eporedillus Cassia Luci liberta Rufa Lucio Decumio Luci filio Rufo pater posuit\n
EDCS-06100235.translation: Lucius Decumius Eporedillus, freedman of Lucius, and Cassia Rufa, freedwoman of Lucius, set this up for Lucius Decumius Rufus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-06100235.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a freedman and freedwoman for their patron or a family member, Lucius Decumius Rufus.

EDCS-07000405.text_edition: \n Luci Vareni Stati fili Arnensis Tauri\n
EDCS-07000405.translation: Lucius Varenus, son of Statu, from Arna, of the tribe Taurine.
EDCS-07000405.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that records the name and tribal affiliation of Lucius Varenus.

EDCS-07000456.text_edition: \n Publius Fabius Quinti filius duovir Caius Umbonius Cai filius Bassus Aulus Livius Auli filius Cursor duoviri tertium ex senatus consulto faciendum curaverunt idemque probaverunt\n
EDCS-07000456.translation: Publius Fabius, son of Quintus, and Caius Umbonius, son of Caius Bassus, and Aulus Livius, son of Aulus Cursor, duovirs, had this made and approved it for the third time by decree of the senate.
EDCS-07000456.summary: This inscription records the completion and approval of a public work by three duovirs, under a senatorial decree.

EDCS-07000457.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Divi filio Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae\n
EDCS-07000457.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the Divine Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, father of the country.
EDCS-07000457.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Augustus, likely from the late 1st century BCE or early 1st century CE.

EDCS-07000459.text_edition: \n Tiberio Claudio Tiberi filio Neroni pontifici consuli patrono\n
EDCS-07000459.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Nero, son of Tiberius, chief priest, consul, patron.
EDCS-07000459.summary: This inscription honors Tiberius Claudius Nero, a prominent figure likely active during the early imperial period.

EDCS-07000460.text_edition: \n Neroni Claudio Tiberi filio Druso auguri praetori urbano patrono\n
EDCS-07000460.translation: To Nero Claudius Drusus, son of Tiberius, augur, urban praetor, patron.
EDCS-07000460.summary: This inscription records the dedication to Nero Claudius Drusus, an augur and urban praetor, probably from the late 1st century BCE or early 1st century CE.

EDCS-07000461.text_edition: \n Marco Agrippai Marci filio Augusti nepoti Postumo\n
EDCS-07000461.translation: To Marcus Agrippa, son of Marcus, grandson of Augustus, Postumus.
EDCS-07000461.summary: This inscription likely refers to Marcus Vipsanius Agrippa Postumus, a grandson of Augustus, active during the late 1st century BCE or early 1st century CE.

EDCS-07000462.text_edition: \n Tiberio Caesari divi Augusti filio Divi Iulii nepoti Augusto consuli IIII tribunicia potestate XXIX imperatori VIII pontifici maximo auguri XVviro sacris faciundis VIIviro epulonum seviri Augustales Marcus Appius Largus Quintus Pinarius Faustus ex honoraria summa decurionum decreto\n
EDCS-07000462.translation: To Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the Divine Augustus, grandson of the Divine Julius, Augustus, consul for the 4th time, holder of tribunician power for the 29th time, emperor for the 8th time, chief priest, augur, member of the college of fifteen for sacred rites, member of the college of seven for feasts; the seven Augustales, Marcus Appius Largus and Quintus Pinarius Faustus, by decree of the decurions, from their distinguished office, have given from their own funds.
EDCS-07000462.summary: This is a public dedication honoring Tiberius Caesar Augustus, likely made by local officials from a colony and dating to his reign (14-37 CE).

EDCS-07000463.text_edition: \n Druso Caesari Tiberi filio Augusti nepoti Divi Iulii pronepoti pontifici consuli designato\n
EDCS-07000463.translation: To Drusus Caesar, son of Tiberius, grandson of Augustus, great-grandson of the Divine Julius, chief priest, designated consul.
EDCS-07000463.summary: This inscription honors Drusus Caesar, son of Tiberius, likely dating to the early 1st century CE.

EDCS-07000465.text_edition: \n Tiberi Claudii Caesaris Augusti Germanici patris patriae \n
EDCS-07000465.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, father of the country.
EDCS-07000465.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Claudius, likely from his reign (41-54 CE).

EDCS-07000467.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Egnatius Cai filius pro praetore\n
EDCS-07000467.translation: Gnaeus Egnatius, son of Caius, former praetor.
EDCS-07000467.summary: This inscription records the name and former office of Gnaeus Egnatius, a former praetor, likely from the late Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-07000468.text_edition: \n filius Collina Martialis IIvir factus pondera sui muneris pecunia sua et i coloniae Felici Luco Feroniae dedit\n
EDCS-07000468.translation: The son of Collina Martialis, having become duumvir, gave the weights for his public duty from his own funds to the colony Felici Luco Feroniae.
EDCS-07000468.summary: This inscription records a donation of weights by a duumvir named Martialis to the colony of Felici Luco Feroniae, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-07000470.text_edition: \n XXI ddominis nnostris Flavio Valerio Constantio et Galerio Valerio Maximiano invictis et clementissimis semper Augustis et ddominis nnostris Flavio Valerio Severo et Galerio Valerio Maximino ac beatissimis Caesaribus\n
EDCS-07000470.translation: To our lords Flavius Valerius Constantius and Galerius Valerius Maximianus, the unconquered and most clement Emperors, and to our lords Flavius Valerius Severus and Galerius Valerius Maximinus and the most blessed Caesars.
EDCS-07000470.summary: This inscription is a dedication to several co-emperors and Caesars, dating to the Tetrarchic period (late 3rd to early 4th century CE).

EDCS-07000472.summary: This is a dedication by Marcus Antonius Chilo, likely on a monument or as a benefactor.

EDCS-07000473.summary: This is a dedication to Caius Aesuleius Donatus, possibly on a tomb or a public monument.

EDCS-07000474.text_edition: \n Divos sic Nerva Marcus Agrippa Luci filius tribunicia potestate V<I> Drusus Caesar Tiberi Augusti filius <divi> Augusti nepos Divi Iuli pronepos tribunicia potestate II T \n
EDCS-07000474.translation: To the deified Nerva, son of Marcus Agrippa, from the tribunician power VI; Drusus Caesar, son of Tiberius Augustus, nephew of the deified Augustus, great-nephew of the deified Julius, from the tribunician power II.
EDCS-07000474.summary: This inscription records the honors bestowed upon emperors Nerva and Drusus Caesar, likely on a public monument, dating to the early imperial period.

EDCS-07400277.text_edition: \n Titus Allianus Titi libertus Bithus aedituus Deanae voto suscepto de suo faciendam curavit libens merito\n
EDCS-07400277.translation: Titus Allianus Bithus, freedman of Titus, temple attendant of Deana, willingly had it made at his own expense, as vowed.
EDCS-07400277.summary: This is a votive offering by Titus Allianus Bithus, a temple attendant of the goddess Deana, likely dedicating a new structure or repair to her shrine.

EDCS-07400278.text_edition: \n Lucius Sicinius Auli filius Arnensis Bellutus IIIIvir iterum iure dicundo Clusi\n
EDCS-07400278.translation: Lucius Sicinius Bellutus, son of Aulus, of the Arnensis tribe, holding the office of IIIIvir for administering justice for the second time at Clusium.
EDCS-07400278.summary: This is an official inscription recording Lucius Sicinius Bellutus's second term as a magistrate in Clusium.

EDCS-07400279.text_edition: \n Lucius Consilius Luci filius Rufus Petronia Natalis\n

EDCS-07400280.text_edition: \n Larcia Luci liberta Hilara Tutili Sminti\n

EDCS-07400289.text_edition: \n Quintus Anilius Quinti libertus Rufio vivos sic fecit\n
EDCS-07400289.translation: Quintus Anilius Rufio, freedman of Quintus, made this while alive.
EDCS-07400289.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Quintus Anilius Rufio for himself during his lifetime, likely marking his burial plot.

EDCS-07400290.text_edition: \n Quintus Anilius Quinti libertus Rufio in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes X\n
EDCS-07400290.translation: Quintus Anilius Rufio, freedman of Quintus, 12 feet in front, 10 feet in the field.
EDCS-07400290.summary: This inscription specifies the dimensions of a plot of land belonging to Quintus Anilius Rufio, likely related to his burial or property.

EDCS-07400291.text_edition: \n Anilia Luci liberta Phaedra Anilia Luci liberta Phaedra\n
EDCS-07400291.translation: Anilia Phaedra, freedwoman of Lucius; Anilia Phaedra, freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-07400291.summary: This inscription seems to record the names of two women, both freedwomen of Lucius, possibly marking shared property or a joint memorial.

EDCS-07400292.text_edition: \n Calvisiae Spuri filiae Danainis in fronte pedes X in agro pedes X\n
EDCS-07400292.translation: To Danainis, daughter of Spurius Calvisius, 10 feet in front, 10 feet in the field.
EDCS-07400292.summary: This inscription likely marks a burial plot of 10x10 feet, dedicated to Danainis, daughter of Spurius Calvisius.

EDCS-07400293.text_edition: \n Quintus Flavius Quinti libertus Diogenes Flavia Quinti liberta Are\n
EDCS-07400293.translation: Quintus Flavius Diogenes, freedman of Quintus; Flavia Are, freedwoman of Quintus.
EDCS-07400293.summary: This inscription records the names of two freedpersons, Quintus Flavius Diogenes and Flavia Are, both freed by a Quintus, possibly marking a property boundary or joint burial.

EDCS-07400294.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Scaudius Cnaei libertus Eumenes in fronte pedes XIIII\n
EDCS-07400294.translation: Cnaeus Scaudius Eumenes, freedman of Cnaeus, 14 feet in front.
EDCS-07400294.summary: This inscription likely marks a burial plot or property boundary of 14 feet in front, belonging to Cnaeus Scaudius Eumenes, a freedman.

EDCS-07400296.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Manio Aquilio Honerato sic vixit annos LI fili bene merenti posuerunt optimo sacerdoti \n
EDCS-07400296.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Manius Aquilius Honeratus, who lived 51 years. His sons placed this for a well-deserving, best priest.
EDCS-07400296.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by the sons for their father, Manius Aquilius Honeratus, who was a priest and lived to be 51 years old.

EDCS-07400299.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely marking the burial place of Lucius Varius.

EDCS-07400300.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cuperius Clemens, of Cuperius.
EDCS-07400300.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Cuperius Clemens.

EDCS-07400301.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valeriae Clementinae Petronius Florus coniugi suae ac carissimae et incumparabili sic fecit quae convixit mecum annis mensibus VIIII diaebus sic XI\n
EDCS-07400301.translation: To the Divine Manes. Petronius Florus made this for his wife Valeria Clementina, his most cherished and incomparable, with whom he lived for 11 years, 9 months, and 11 days.
EDCS-07400301.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Petronius Florus for his beloved wife Valeria Clementina, detailing the length of their marriage.

EDCS-07600180.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Curiatio Restituto vixit annos II menses IIII\n
EDCS-07600180.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Curiatio Restitutus lived for 2 years and 4 months.
EDCS-07600180.summary: A funerary dedication for Curiatio Restitutus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-07600181.text_edition: \n Minucia Fausta vixit annos XL Caius Carfenius Va matri\n
EDCS-07600181.translation: Minucia Fausta lived for 40 years. Caius Carfenius dedicated this to his mother.
EDCS-07600181.summary: A funerary dedication for Minucia Fausta, recording her age at death and identifying the dedicator as her son.

EDCS-08300274.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Nervae filio Nervae Traiano Augusto Germanico Dacico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate X imperatori V consuli V patri patriae restitutori coloniae Iuliae Felicis Lucus Feroniae publice ex decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-08300274.translation: For the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Nerva, Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 10th time, imperator for the 5th time, consul for the 5th time, father of the country, restorer of the colony Iulia Felix of Lucus Feroniae, publicly, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08300274.summary: A public dedication honouring Emperor Trajan, likely for his achievements and his restoration of the colony of Lucus Feroniae, dated by his tribunician and consular powers.

EDCS-08300289.text_edition: \n Pro salute Quinti Nunni Apolloni familiae eius et suae Silvano sacrum posuit Antonius servus\n
EDCS-08300289.translation: For the health of Quintus Nunnius Apollonius and his family, freeborn and slave. Antonius, a slave, set this up as sacred to Silvanus.
EDCS-08300289.summary: A dedication to the god Silvanus for the health of Quintus Nunnius Apollonius and his household, set up by a slave named Antonius.

EDCS-08400038.text_edition: \n Comiciae Doxsae sic conditivom sic Comiciae Doxsae sic Caius Murrius Pastor coniugi sanctissimae et carissimae in agro pedes XV in fronte pedes XV Comiciae Doxsae sic Caius Murrius Pastor coniugi sanctissimae et carissimae in agro pedes XV in fronte pedes XV\n
EDCS-08400038.translation: For Comicia Doxsa, thus buried, thus Comicia Doxsa. Caius Murrius Pastor to his most holy and dearest wife, in the field 15 feet, in the front 15 feet. Comicia Doxsa, thus Comicia Doxsa. Caius Murrius Pastor to his most holy and dearest wife, in the field 15 feet, in the front 15 feet.
EDCS-08400038.summary: A funerary dedication for Comicia Doxsa, specifying the dimensions of her burial plot, made by her husband Caius Murrius Pastor.

EDCS-08400233.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Agesilai fecit Quintus Viriusiusntamus patri bene merenti \n
EDCS-08400233.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Viriusiusntamus made this for his deserving father Agesilaus.
EDCS-08400233.summary: A funerary dedication for Agesilaus, made by his son Quintus Viriusiusntamus.

EDCS-08400235.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Herenniae Helpidi quae vixit annos X \n
EDCS-08400235.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Herennia Helpidis, who lived for 10 years.
EDCS-08400235.summary: A funerary dedication for Herennia Helpidis, recording her age at death.

EDCS-08400236.text_edition: \n Lucceiae Vitali matri fecerunt Trebenna mus et Lucceia Sabina fili et - Lucceius Sabinus maritus uxori --- \n
EDCS-08400236.translation: For their mother Luccia Vitalis, Trebenus and Luccia Sabina, children, and Luccius Sabinus, husband, made this for his wife.
EDCS-08400236.summary: A funerary dedication for Luccia Vitalis, made by her children and husband.

EDCS-08400237.text_edition: \n libertus Eros Euhemeris s libertus Auctus ste ia ta Postumiae Luci |(mulieris) libertae Lezbiae Luci Postumi |(mulieris) liberti Celeris L(uci)? Postumi Luci |(mulieris) liberti Saturnini\n
EDCS-08400237.translation: Freedman Eros, freedman of Euhemeris, freedman of Actus, [perhaps slave] of Postumia Lucia, freedwoman of Lucius Postumius, freedman of Lucius Postumius, freedman of Saturninus.
EDCS-08400237.summary: A complex funerary inscription detailing the manumission and relationships of several freedmen and freedwomen connected to the Postumia family.

EDCS-08400238.translation: Titinius Petrinianus gives this gift to Luna.
EDCS-08400238.summary: A dedication of a gift to the goddess Luna by Titinius Petrinianus.

EDCS-08500274.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Venuleius Apronianus.

EDCS-08500276.translation: Terentia Hermione, fellow freedwoman of Nauta.
EDCS-08500276.summary: This epitaph identifies Terentia Hermione as a freedwoman.

EDCS-08500278.text_edition: \n Publius Hirtius Titi filius Publius Curfidius filius Marcus Fuficius filius Salvius Vibidius filius magistri \n
EDCS-08500278.translation: Publius Hirtius, son of Titus; Publius Curfidius, son of Publius; Marcus Fuficius, son of Publius; Salvius Vibidius, son of Marcus, masters.
EDCS-08500278.summary: This inscription lists four individuals, likely holding magistracies or important roles, possibly related to a public building or function.

EDCS-08500281.text_edition: \n Aulus Lucius Seii Auli filii curatores aquae ex aere conlato Fonti Telluri sacrum\n
EDCS-08500281.translation: Aulus Lucius and Seius, sons of Aulus, curators of the water, sacred to Fontus and Tellus from money collected.
EDCS-08500281.summary: This is a dedication to the gods Fontus and Tellus by water curators who collected funds for the work, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08500283.text_edition: \n Lucius Octavius Auli filius IIvir de sua pecunia fecit forumque refecit\n
EDCS-08500283.translation: Lucius Octavius, son of Aulus, as *duovir*, made this at his own expense and rebuilt the forum.
EDCS-08500283.summary: This inscription records Lucius Octavius, a *duovir*, who funded the construction and repair of the forum, likely from the 1st century CE.

EDCS-08500286.translation: Caesia Felicitas, while you are alive with your people.
EDCS-08500286.summary: This is a brief, possibly funerary or dedicatory, inscription to Caesia Felicitas.

EDCS-08600215.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Caius Iulius Verus Maximinus Invictus Augustus et Caius Iulius Verus Maximus nobilissimus Caesar opus porticus fori et aedibus cum hodio sic vetustate dilapsum pecunia publica Cosanorum restitui iusserunt curam agente Caio Rufio Cai filio Proculo clarissimo viro\n
EDCS-08600215.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Invictus Augustus and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, ordered the work of the portico of the forum and the buildings, which had fallen into ruin through age, to be restored with public money of the Cosani, under the care of Gaius Rufius Proculus, son of Gaius Rufius, a most distinguished man.
EDCS-08600215.summary: This imperial inscription records Maximinus I and his son, Caesar Maximus, ordering the restoration of a forum portico and buildings in Cosanum, likely dated to their joint rule (c. 236-238 CE).

EDCS-08600216.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ianuarius Ianuario filio pientissimo iudicio decessori suo vixit nnis XXVIII mensibus XI diebus XX praeter bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-08600216.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Ianuarius made this for his most dutiful son, Ianuarius, his predecessor, who lived 28 years, 11 months, and 20 days, and for his well-deserving service.
EDCS-08600216.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Ianuarius for his son, also named Ianuarius, who died at the age of 28 years, 11 months, and 20 days, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08600217.text_edition: \n Sextus Marius Luci filius Sabatina Montanus eques Romanus Veratia Cai filia Aufidia uxor Caius Marius Sexti filius Aufidianus equo publico vixit nnos XIIII\n
EDCS-08600217.translation: Sextus Marius Montanus, son of Lucius, of the Sabatine tribe, Roman knight; Veratia Aufidia, daughter of Gaius, his wife; Gaius Marius Aufidianus, son of Sextus, lived 14 years with public equestrian status.
EDCS-08600217.summary: This epitaph records Sextus Marius Montanus, a Roman knight, his wife Veratia Aufidia, and their son Gaius Marius Aufidianus, who died at the age of 14.

EDCS-08600219.translation: To Cornelia Sympherusa, daughter of Quintus.
EDCS-08600219.summary: This is a dedication or epitaph to Cornelia Sympherusa.

EDCS-08600220.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quinti Caecili Celeris et Naeviae Iadis\n
EDCS-08600220.translation: To the divine spirits of Quintus Caecilius Celer and Naevia Iadis.
EDCS-08600220.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the divine spirits of two individuals, Quintus Caecilius Celer and Naevia Iadis.

EDCS-08600227.translation: Gracchus L. V. / Gracchus L. V.
EDCS-08600227.summary: This inscription appears to record the name Gracchus, possibly with numerical designations or abbreviations, repeated twice.

EDCS-08600231.text_edition: \n XXXIV PPubliorum Persi Persi et Luci Aeli Pri Luci Aeli\n
EDCS-08600231.translation: 34 years, of the Publii, Persus and Lucius Aelius, son of Persus and Lucius Aelius.
EDCS-08600231.summary: This inscription records a duration of 34 years and names individuals from the Publii family, likely related to age or tenure.

EDCS-08600233.text_edition: \n Agatinia Prima Luci Gavi Celsi XXIIII Ligur\n
EDCS-08600233.translation: Agatinia Prima, 24 years, daughter of Lucius G., Ligur.
EDCS-08600233.summary: This inscription records the name Agatinia Prima, her age of 24, her parentage from Lucius G., and her origin or cognomen 'Ligur'.

EDCS-08600238.translation: Pri Lae, son of Crispus Brocchus.
EDCS-08600238.summary: This inscription records the name Pri Lae and identifies his father as Crispus Brocchus.

EDCS-08600241.text_edition: \n Caio Arminio Caecina Caecinia Armini Paetus \n
EDCS-08600241.translation: To Gaius Arminius, son of Caecina, Caecinia, daughter of Arminius Paetus.
EDCS-08600241.summary: This inscription records names likely indicating family relationships: Gaius Arminius, Caecina, and Caecinia, daughter of Arminius Paetus.

EDCS-08600242.translation: Of Laelius Brocchus, of Laelius Crispus.
EDCS-08600242.summary: This inscription records two names, Laelius Brocchus and Laelius Crispus, possibly indicating ownership or dedication.

EDCS-08600243.text_edition: \n tesserario legionis Alliae S App filiae Herennius Secundus Herenni Secun(di) filius? \n
EDCS-08600243.translation: To Herennius Secundus, son of Herennius Secundus, standard-bearer of the legion, son of Allia, daughter of S., appointed pontiff.
EDCS-08600243.summary: This inscription dedicates a monument to Herennius Secundus, a legionary standard-bearer and pontiff, also mentioning his father and mother.

EDCS-08600244.text_edition: \n Aulus Tutilius Cai filius Statiena Quinti filia Prisca huic bene compositae lucem fo apstulit sic immiti pernic et quam rite viro iunxit hanc prior infelix Lucius Arrecinus ann Lucius Tutilius Cai filius Sabatina Modestus\n
EDCS-08600244.translation: Aulus Tutilius, son of Caius, from the Statiena tribe, Prisca, daughter of Quintus, from the Sabatina tribe, joined this well-arranged tomb with her husband; Lucius Arrecinus, in his unhappy [life], took away the light from her; Lucius Tutilius, son of Caius, from the Sabatina tribe, Modestus.
EDCS-08600244.summary: This funerary inscription records Aulus Tutilius, his wife Prisca, and her brother Lucius Tutilius Modestus, lamenting the premature death of Prisca and blaming Lucius Arrecinus.

EDCS-08600245.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marci Anaeni Marci fili Sabatina Phariani equo publico ex V decuriis pontificis quaestoris aedilis IIIIviri iure dicundo quinquennalis ex testamento ipsius curam agente Autumnale liberto quisque viator transis et hunc titulum leges dices Anaeni Phariane sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08600245.translation: To the divine spirits of Marcus Anaenius, son of Marcus, from the Sabatina tribe, Pharianus, a public horseman from the V decuriae, pontiff, quaestor, aedile, IIIIvir for administering justice, quinquennalis; by his will, while Autumnalis, his freedman, managed the care, may each traveler who passes and reads this inscription say, 'Anaenius Pharianus, may the earth be light for you'.
EDCS-08600245.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Anaenius Pharianus, detailing his distinguished public career and containing a posthumous wish for travelers to bless his grave.

EDCS-08600246.text_edition: \n m quinquennali curatori negotiantium vinariorumium concessum ae Felicitati coniugi Rhodine et Aemilio ccio Severo filio libertis libertabusque \n
EDCS-08600246.translation: Granted by M. by quinquennial status, curator of the wine merchants, to Felicitas, his wife Rhodinē, and to Aemilius Severus, his freedman and his freedwomen.
EDCS-08600246.summary: This inscription records a grant made by a curator of wine merchants to his wife, son, and freedpersons.

EDCS-08600247.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Petronius Cai filius Galeria Felix publicus haruspex Varronia Cai liberta Fortunata hic adquiescunt parentes piissimi\n
EDCS-08600247.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Petronius Felix, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, public haruspex, and Varronia Fortunata, freedwoman of Gaius, rest here, most dutiful parents.
EDCS-08600247.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a public haruspex and his freedwoman wife, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08900328.text_edition: \n Iano Patri sacrum Lucius Titinius Vitalis sevir Augustalis et Lucius Titinius Luci filius Pelagianus Arnensis aedilis quaestor rei publicae haruspices ex voto posuerunt\n
EDCS-08900328.translation: Sacred to Father Janus. Lucius Titinius Vitalis, Augustalis sevir and Lucius Titinius Pelagianus, son of Lucius, Arnensis aedile, quaestor of the republic, haruspices, set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-08900328.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Janus by two haruspices who held various civic offices, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08900332.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Beronice Sotericus coniugi bene merenti fecit vixit annis XXV\n
EDCS-08900332.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Sotericus made this for Beronice, his deserving wife, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-08900332.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Beronice, commemorating her life and made by her husband.

EDCS-08900333.text_edition: \n S RDIAnesis et Vitalis ponendum curaverunt Photis Hilarius Hypora par(entibus bene merentibus faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-08900333.translation: Anesis and Vitalis saw to the setting up, Photis and Hilarius and Hypora saw to the making for their deserving parents.
EDCS-08900333.summary: This inscription records individuals who erected and constructed a monument for their deserving parents.

EDCS-08900339.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelia Sexti filia DI Dido Quintus Rutilius \n
EDCS-08900339.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aelia Dido, daughter of Sextus, and Quintus Rutilius [---]
EDCS-08900339.summary: This inscription is a fragmented funerary epitaph for Aelia Dido and Quintus Rutilius.

EDCS-08900344.text_edition: \n Germanico Caesari Tiberi Augusti filio d[ivi Augusti nepoti ------\n
EDCS-08900344.translation: For Germanicus Caesar, son of Tiberius Augustus, grandson of the deified Augustus [---]
EDCS-08900344.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Germanicus Caesar, son of Tiberius, and grandson of Augustus, likely set up by public funds.

EDCS-08900346.text_edition: \n Druso Caesari Germanici Caesaris filio Tiberi Caesaris Augusti nepoti divi Augusti pronepoti ex decreto decurionum pecunia publica\n
EDCS-08900346.translation: For Drusus Caesar, son of Germanicus Caesar, grandson of Tiberius Caesar Augustus, great-grandson of the deified Augustus, by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-08900346.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Drusus Caesar, funded by public money following a decree of the local council.

EDCS-08900348.text_edition: \n Pecunia publica Caius Lisinius Firmus Quintus Afonas Aco Clemens IIviri\n
EDCS-08900348.translation: With public money. Gaius Lisinius Firmus and Quintus Afonas Clemens, the two IIviri.
EDCS-08900348.summary: This inscription records an action taken with public money by two duoviri, likely a form of local magistracy.

EDCS-08900349.text_edition: \n Voto suscepto pro salute et reditu et victoria Britannica Tiberi Claudi Caesaris Augusti Germanici pontificis maximi tribunicia potestate V imperatoris X patris patriae consulis designati IIII Aulus Vicirius Proculus flamen Augustalis tribunus militum Victoriae Britannicae votum solvit\n
EDCS-08900349.translation: Having undertaken a vow for the safety, return, and victory in Britain of Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power X, imperator X, father of the country, consul-designate IIII, Aulus Vicirius Proculus, flamen Augustalis, military tribune, fulfilled his vow to Victoria Britannica.
EDCS-08900349.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Aulus Vicirius Proculus, a priest and military official, to the personification of victory in Britain, likely during the reign of Claudius.

EDCS-08900350.text_edition: \n Ex voto suscepto pro salute Tiberi Claudi Caesaris Augusti fili Brittannici Aulus Vicirius Auli filius Proculus tribunus militum flamen Augustalis posuit\n
EDCS-08900350.translation: In fulfillment of a vow made for the health of Tiberius Claudius Caesar Britannicus, son of Augustus, Aulus Vicirius Proculus, son of Aulus, military tribune and Augustalis flamen, erected this.
EDCS-08900350.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication made by Aulus Vicirius Proculus, a military tribune and flamen, for the health of Britannicus, likely from the 1st century AD.

EDCS-08900351.text_edition: \n C Lucius Alfius Po seviri P\n
EDCS-08900351.translation: Lucius Alfius, a freedman, member of the college of Seviri.
EDCS-08900351.summary: This is a very brief inscription identifying Lucius Alfius, possibly a freedman and member of the Seviri, with no date or further context.

EDCS-08900353.text_edition: \n Virius Publi filius Oufentina cundus miles cohortis I praetoriae vixsit sic annis XXXI militavit annis X \n
EDCS-08900353.translation: Vibius, son of Publius, of the Oufentina tribe, a soldier of the I Praetorian cohort, lived 31 years, served 10 years.
EDCS-08900353.summary: This epitaph records the life and military service of Vibius, a soldier in the Praetorian Guard, who died at age 31 after 10 years of service, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-08900355.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vibiae Auli filiae Romulae memoriae eius sanctae quae vixit annos XVII menses X Vibius Romulus et Vibia Priscilla parentes filiae \n
EDCS-08900355.translation: To the Divine Shades. In memory of his devout wife, Romula, daughter of Aulus, who lived 17 years and 10 months. Vibius Romulus and Vibia Priscilla, parents, (made this) for their daughter.
EDCS-08900355.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Vibius Romulus and Vibia Priscilla for their daughter Romula, who died at the age of 17 years and 10 months, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-08900356.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum memoriae Suilliae Secundae Secundus et Nemusa filiae fecerunt vixit annos XII\n
EDCS-08900356.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Secundus and Nemusa made this in memory of their daughter Suillia Secunda, who lived 12 years.
EDCS-08900356.summary: This epitaph records a funerary dedication made by Secundus and Nemusa for their daughter Suillia Secunda, who died at the age of 12, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-08900358.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Sentiae Cai filiae Severinae coniugi carissimae Lucius Valerius Primitivus cun sic qua convixit annis plus minus XXXV sine quaerella sic bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-08900358.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Valerius Primitivus made this for his dearest wife Severina, daughter of Caius, with whom he lived for more or less 35 years, without complaint, a well-deserving woman.
EDCS-08900358.summary: This funerary dedication was made by Lucius Valerius Primitivus for his wife Severina, honoring their marriage of approximately 35 years, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-08900366.text_edition: \n Sacrum Nymphis Athenio Caesaris nostri vilicus posuit cum suis sub cura Titi Cavi Amerimni\n
EDCS-08900366.translation: Sacred to the Nymphs. Athenio, steward of our Caesar, erected this with his household, under the supervision of Titus Cavius Amerimnus.
EDCS-08900366.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Nymphs made by Athenio, a steward of the emperor, and his household, supervised by Titus Cavius Amerimnus, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-08900903.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Donata.
EDCS-08900903.summary: This is a very brief funerary dedication to the Divine Shades for a person named Donata, with no further context or date.

EDCS-08900905.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Primitio.
EDCS-08900905.summary: This is a very brief funerary dedication to the Divine Shades for a person named Primitio, with no further context or date.

EDCS-08900906.translation: Sextus Vettius Diogenes, freedman of Sextus.
EDCS-08900906.summary: This inscription simply identifies Sextus Vettius Diogenes as a freedman of Sextus, with no further information or date.

EDCS-08900907.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Octavius |(mulieris) libertus Nireus Longidia Publi liberta Lais\n
EDCS-08900907.translation: Cnaeus Octavius Nireus, freedman of the woman, and Lais, freedwoman of Longidia Publius.
EDCS-08900907.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the freedmen of Cnaeus Octavius and Longidia Publius, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08900909.text_edition: \n Publius Aescionius Publi filius Aemilia Numisius fecit\n
EDCS-08900909.translation: Publius Aescionius Numisius, son of Publius, of the Aemilia tribe, made this.
EDCS-08900909.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Publius Aescionius Numisius for himself or a relative, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08900910.text_edition: \n Marcia |(mulieris) liberta Olympia Marcia |(mulieris) liberta Secunda liberta Lucius Marcius |(mulieris) libertus Celadus libertus Marcia |(mulieris) liberta Methe liberta Lucius Marcius |(mulieris) libertus Gemellus libertus\n
EDCS-08900910.translation: Olympia, freedwoman of Marcia, and Secunda, freedwoman of Marcia, freedwoman Lucius Marcius, and Celadus, freedman of Lucius Marcius, and Methe, freedwoman of Lucius Marcius, and Gemellus, freedman of Lucius Marcius.
EDCS-08900910.summary: This is a funerary inscription listing several freedmen and freedwomen of Marcia and Lucius Marcius, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08900912.text_edition: \n Laurenti Lucio Licinio Fabia Iuliano equiti Romano patrono et curatori rei publicae Foroclodiensium praefecturae Claudiae Laurenti Lavinati aedili et praetore sic urbis Lavini sacerdoti Martis Gradivi curatori viae Curtiae patrono coloniae Carsiolanorum decuriones populusque Foroclodiensium Imperatore Severo Alexandro Augusto III et Cassio Dione II consulibus curantibus Lucio Cestio Arreuctemone Caio Iulio Sabiniano\n
EDCS-08900912.translation: To Laurentius, son of Lucius, Julian, of the Fabia tribe, Roman knight, patron and curator of the public affairs of Foroclodii, prefect of the Claudian prefecture of Lavinium, aedile and praetor of the city of Lavinium, priest of Mars Gradivus, curator of the Curtian Way, patron of the colony of Carsiolani, by the decurions and people of Foroclodii, in the consulship of Emperor Severus Alexander Augustus for the 3rd time and Cassius Dio for the 2nd time, under the supervision of Lucius Cestius Arreuctemon and Gaius Julius Sabinianus.
EDCS-08900912.summary: This is a public dedication by the people of Foroclodii to Laurentius Julian, honoring his various public offices and patronages, dated to the consulship of Severus Alexander and Cassius Dio (229 CE).

EDCS-08900915.text_edition: \n Marcus Lucilius Spuri filius Maximus fecit Luciliae Spuri filiae Secundae matri suae bene merenti\n
EDCS-08900915.translation: Marcus Lucilius Maximus, son of Spurius, made this for his mother Lucilia Secunda, daughter of Spurius, deserving of his regard.
EDCS-08900915.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Marcus Lucilius Maximus for his mother Lucilia Secunda, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08900916.text_edition: \n Diis Manibus Publi Arrani Publi fili Secundi Marci Coli Pothi Augustalis Annaeas Cipasi sic servae\n
EDCS-08900916.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Arranius Secundus, son of Publius, of the Cornelia tribe, and Pothus Augustalis, son of Marcus Colius, and Annaeas Cipasius, slave woman.
EDCS-08900916.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes, listing several individuals including Publius Arranius Secundus, Marcus Colius Pothus, and Annaeas Cipasius, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08900917.translation: Zoe, mother, dedicated this gift to Liber.
EDCS-08900917.summary: This is a dedication of a gift to the god Liber by a woman named Zoe, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08900918.text_edition: \n RPI vir iure dicundo Corneliae posiit sic\n
EDCS-08900918.translation: Cornelia dedicated this in the exercise of her right of jurisdiction.
EDCS-08900918.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Cornelia in her official capacity, likely related to a legal or administrative function, possibly from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08900921.text_edition: \n Clemens Aug(usti) libertus proc(urator) Claudio Hieroni parenti b(ene) merenti fecit\n
EDCS-08900921.translation: Clemens, freedman of Augustus, procurator, made this for Claudius Hiero, deserving of his regard.
EDCS-08900921.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Clemens, an imperial freedman and procurator, for Claudius Hiero, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08900926.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Visinio Libaerali sic Marcus Visinius Crescens patri bene merenti\n
EDCS-08900926.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Visinius Crescens for his deserving father Marcus Visinius Libaeralis.
EDCS-08900926.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Marcus Visinius Crescens for his father Marcus Visinius Libaeralis, dedicated to the Divine Manes, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08900929.text_edition: \n Memoriae Petroniae Apronianenis qui sic vixit annis XXXII mensibus X dies sic XV coniugi karissimae bene merenti\n
EDCS-08900929.translation: To the memory of Petronia Apronianenis, who lived 32 years, 10 months, and 15 days, to my dearest, well-deserving wife.
EDCS-08900929.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age and dedication of Petronia Apronianenis by her husband.

EDCS-08900931.text_edition: \n Aulo Atilio Luci filio Glabrioni IIIIviro municipes et incolae\n
EDCS-08900931.translation: To Aulus Atilius Glabrio, son of Lucius, by the four magistrates and the inhabitants.
EDCS-08900931.summary: This inscription honors Aulus Atilius Glabrio, likely a local official, by the local community.

EDCS-09001300.text_edition: \n Spei Augustae sacrum Marcus Laterius Modestus sevir Augustalis de sua pecunia dedit dedicavit CADPP\n
EDCS-09001300.translation: Sacred to Imperial Hope. Marcus Laterius Modestus, Augustalis Seviri, dedicated this from his own funds. CADPP
EDCS-09001300.summary: This is a dedication to 'Imperial Hope' by Marcus Laterius Modestus, a member of the Augustales, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09001301.text_edition: \n Iuliae Mamae<ae> Augustae matri Imperatoris Severi Alexandri Pii Felicis Augusti res publica Hebanorum decreto decurionum ponendam curavit\n
EDCS-09001301.translation: The state of Hebanum, by decree of the decurions, arranged for this to be erected to Julia Mamaea Augusta, mother of Emperor Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-09001301.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument to Julia Mamaea, mother of Severus Alexander, by the town council of Hebanum.

EDCS-09001302.text_edition: \n Diis Manibus sacrum Publio Fabio Menae Aemilia Terpusa coniugi optimo fecit\n
EDCS-09001302.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aemilia Terpusa made this for her most excellent husband, Publius Fabio Mena.
EDCS-09001302.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Aemilia Terpusa for her deceased husband, Publius Fabio Mena.

EDCS-09001303.text_edition: \n Bonae Deae sanctae Maecia Rennia Fuscianilla et Iulia Profutura resituerunt\n
EDCS-09001303.translation: Maecia Rennia Fuscianilla and Iulia Profutura restored (this monument) to the holy Good Goddess.
EDCS-09001303.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a sanctuary of the Good Goddess by Maecia Rennia Fuscianilla and Iulia Profutura.

EDCS-09001304.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Cornelius Priscus Valerinus consul fecit\n
EDCS-09001304.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Publius Cornelius Priscus Valerinus, consul, made this.
EDCS-09001304.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Publius Cornelius Priscus Valerinus, who held the consulship, likely dating to the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-09001306.text_edition: \n Naevio Cai filio IIIIviro tribuno militum\n
EDCS-09001306.translation: To Naevius Caius, son of Caius, four-magistrate, military tribune.
EDCS-09001306.summary: This inscription honors Naevius Caius, likely a local official and military officer, possibly on a tombstone or commemorative monument.

EDCS-09001308.text_edition: \n Gaius Larcius Gai filius Diogenes in fronte pedes XIII in agro pedes \n
EDCS-09001308.translation: Gaius Larcius Diogenes, son of Gaius, in the front 13 feet, in the field [---] feet.
EDCS-09001308.summary: This inscription appears to record land measurements, possibly for a property boundary or a funerary plot, belonging to Gaius Larcius Diogenes.

EDCS-09001309.text_edition: \n S C sacrum Restitutus p p libens votum solvit\n
EDCS-09001309.translation: By senatorial decree, Restitutus, public benefactor, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09001309.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Restitutus, a public benefactor, likely in response to a senatorial decree.

EDCS-09001310.text_edition: \n Domitiae Priscillae quae vixit nnos XVIIII dies RASC fecit\n
EDCS-09001310.translation: Priscilla made this for Domitia, who lived 19 years.
EDCS-09001310.summary: A funerary dedication for Domitia Priscilla, who died at 19.

EDCS-09001311.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gaio Allio Legitimo Allius Maximus Allius Marcellianus fratri piissimo fecerunt vixit annos XXVII menses IIII dies VII\n
EDCS-09001311.translation: Allius Maximus and Allius Marcellianus made this for their most devoted brother Gaius Allius Legitimus, who lived 27 years, 4 months, and 7 days.
EDCS-09001311.summary: A funerary dedication from brothers for Gaius Allius Legitimus, who died at age 27.

EDCS-09001313.summary: The name of an individual, possibly a tombstone inscription.

EDCS-09001314.text_edition: \n Tuccia Cnaei liberta Salvia fecit sibi et Cnaeo Tuccio Cnaei liberto Apollonio patrono suo et Lucio Aburnio Iucundo et LIO et ARIN matri\n
EDCS-09001314.translation: Salvia, Tuccia's freedwoman, made this for herself, for her patron Cnaeus Tuccius Apollonius, a freedman of Cnaeus, and for Lucius Aburnius Iucundus, and LIO, and ARIN, and her mother.
EDCS-09001314.summary: A funerary dedication made by a freedwoman for herself, her patron, and other family members.

EDCS-09001315.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Laniae Victoriae Severus coniugi karissimae bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-09001315.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. Severus made this for his most beloved and deserving wife, Lania Victora.
EDCS-09001315.summary: A funerary dedication from a husband for his wife, Lania Victora.

EDCS-09001316.text_edition: \n Caio Rosio Cai filio Arnensi equiti Turraniae Cai filiae uxori Caio Rosio Cai fili filio Arnensi Rosio Cai fili filio Arnensi Sabino tribuno militum legionis IIII heredes? faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-09001316.translation: The heirs made this for Gaius Rosius Arnensis, a Roman knight, for his wife Turrania, daughter of Gaius, for their son Gaius Rosius Arnensis, and for their son Sabinus, a military tribune of the Fourth Legion.
EDCS-09001316.summary: A funerary dedication for multiple members of the Rosius family, including a Roman knight and a military tribune.

EDCS-09001317.translation: To the divine spirits, for Victor, who deserved well.

EDCS-09001319.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Sulpiciae Charidis Servi Sulpici Augusti liberti Clementis numunclatoris sic liberti heredes posuerunt\n
EDCS-09001319.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. The heirs of Servus Sulpicius Clemens, freedman of Sulpicius Augustus, the scribe, placed this for Sulpicia Charis.
EDCS-09001319.summary: A funerary dedication for Sulpicia Charis, placed by the heirs of her freedman.

EDCS-09001320.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulio Pastori IIR succor Aug vixit annos III menses VIIII dies VIIII Iulius Priscus pater filio piissimo fecit\n
EDCS-09001320.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. Gaius Julius Priscus made this for his most devoted son, Iulius Pastor, helper of the emperor, who lived 3 years, 9 months, and 9 days.
EDCS-09001320.summary: A funerary dedication from a father for his young son, Iulius Pastor.

EDCS-09001323.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Hostiliae Dlae Lucius Hostilius \n
EDCS-09001323.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits. Lucius Hostilius [made this for] Hostilia Dla.
EDCS-09001323.summary: A funerary dedication from Lucius Hostilius for Hostilia Dla.

EDCS-09001324.translation: Eutyches to his most dutiful mother.
EDCS-09001324.summary: A funerary dedication by Eutyches to his mother.

EDCS-09200053.text_edition: \n Laribus sacrum Marcus M Rosa Aulus Luci libertus Rufus donum dant\n
EDCS-09200053.translation: Sacred to the Lares. Marcus M. Rosa and Aulus Rufus, freedman of Lucius, give this offering.
EDCS-09200053.summary: A dedication to the Lares by Marcus M. Rosa and Aulus Rufus.

EDCS-09200054.text_edition: \n Titus Postumius Titi libertus Diphilus Furia Luci liberta Rufa Equitia Quinti filia Rufilla\n
EDCS-09200054.translation: Titus Postumius Diphilus, freedman of Titus, and Furia Rufilla, daughter of Quitus, freedwoman of Lucius, and Equitia [daughter of Quitus?].
EDCS-09200054.summary: A list of names, possibly related to a funerary or property transaction, with familial and freed status indicated.

EDCS-09200055.text_edition: \n Vixit anno uno Leontis anima Flaviae Meliteniae filiae dulcissimae Marci Aureli Flavi Mu Meliteniae\n
EDCS-09200055.translation: Lived one year, Leontis, soul of Flavia Melitenia, dearest daughter of Marcus Aurelius Flavi Mu and Melitenia.
EDCS-09200055.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Leontis, daughter of Flavia Melitenia and Marcus Aurelius Flavi Mu.

EDCS-09200248.text_edition: \n In honorem domus divinae Publius Sestius Publi libertus Corumbus sevir Augustalis ex pecunia sua et honoraria ex decreto decurionum Lucio Cornelio Sulla Felice Lucio Livio Ocella Sulpicio Galba consulibus\n
EDCS-09200248.translation: In honor of the divine house, Publius Sestius Corumbus, freedman of Publius, Augustalis sevir, at his own expense and with honorary funds, by decree of the decurions, during the consulship of Lucius Cornelius Sulla Felix and Lucius Livius Ocella Sulpicius Galba.
EDCS-09200248.summary: An honorary inscription recording a public works project funded by Publius Sestius Corumbus, an Augustalis sevir, likely during the late 1st century AD or early 2nd century AD, given the consuls.

EDCS-09200249.text_edition: \n Marcus Met fistul fistula tricenaria a castello ad castellum quod est at sic E pedes CCLXX castellum ad theatrum fistula a castello theatri d in balneo ad castellum quod est pedes CCXXIIII fistula denaria a castello basilicam cum I item ad lacum c fistula senaria pro fistula NRIQRI CCXXC theatri jj n CCIX CLXIIX erva quom sic XCIIII ------] osa rivi pedes VII Herculis quod est II II pedes upo regularis rivi pedes CV pedes pedes VI pedes XXX XVS tabulario CXV MAS I pedes CXXXVI ri regular--- XV pedes XC XV DCCCXV semis O DXXCI semis XXIV NP pedes DXLI CCLXI semis\n
EDCS-09200249.translation: Marcus Met [provides?] a thirty-unit pipe from one fort to another, which is at [location name?], 270 feet; a pipe from the fort at the theater to the bath, which is [located?] 224 feet; a ten-unit pipe from the fort to the Basilica with I [pipes?]; also to the lake, a six-unit pipe, instead of pipe [damaged] 220, theater jj n 209, 168; servant, which is thus 94 ------] flowing stream, 7 feet; Hercules, which is II II, feet; upo regular, stream, 105 feet, feet, 6 feet, 30 feet XVS tabularium 115 MAS I, feet 136 ri regular--- 15 feet, 90, 15, 800-815, half O 5291, half 24 NP, feet 541, 261, half.
EDCS-09200249.summary: A complex inscription detailing the lengths and types of pipes for water distribution, likely related to a municipal water system, including distances from various points and possibly noting repairs or new installations, with significant lacunae.

EDCS-09200250.text_edition: \n Quinti Volusi Saturnini Caius Herennius Vitalis fecit\n
EDCS-09200250.summary: A dedication or commission of a work by Caius Herennius Vitalis.

EDCS-09200251.translation: The workshop of Quitus Corellius Proculus.
EDCS-09200251.summary: An inscription identifying the workshop of Quitus Corellius Proculus.

EDCS-09200252.summary: A dedication or commission of a work by Aulus Vicirius Augurinus.

EDCS-09200256.text_edition: \n Lucius Suedius Bassus Caius Masurius Capito IIviri aquam Augustam restituendam et ampliandam novis capitibus et rivis ex decreto decurionum curaverunt\n
EDCS-09200256.translation: Lucius Suedius Bassus and Caius Masurius Capito, the duoviri, took care of restoring and enlarging the Augustan aqueduct with new channels and streams, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-09200256.summary: An inscription recording the restoration and expansion of the aqueduct by the duoviri Lucius Suedius Bassus and Caius Masurius Capito, ordered by the decurions, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-09200257.text_edition: \n Lucio Volusio Quinti filio Saturnino consuli VIIviro epulonum ex decreto decurionum publice patrono\n
EDCS-09200257.translation: To Lucius Volusius Saturninus, son of Quintus, consul, VIIvir of the epulones, as patron, by public decree of the council of decurions.
EDCS-09200257.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Volusius Saturninus, honoring his consulship and public patronage, decreed by local officials.

EDCS-09200258.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publio Comicio Caletrano veterano ex praetorio vixit annos XLI Avedia Severa coniugi bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-09200258.translation: To the departed spirits. For Publius Comicius Caletranus, a veteran from the Praetorian guard, who lived 41 years. Avedia Severa made this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-09200258.summary: A tombstone for Publius Comicius Caletranus, a veteran of the Praetorian guard, erected by his wife.

EDCS-09200259.text_edition: \n Ecutio Cai filio Sabatina vixsit sic annos XXV Valeria Edumele mater filio posuit Ecutius Cai filius Sabatina Passer frater eius est\n
EDCS-09200259.translation: To Ecutius, son of Caius, of the Sabatina tribe. He lived 25 years. His mother, Valeria Edumele, placed this for her son. Ecutius, son of Caius, called Passer, his brother, is also here.
EDCS-09200259.summary: A tombstone for Ecutius, son of Caius, erected by his mother, with mention of his brother.

EDCS-09200260.text_edition: \n Lucius Alius Cai filius annorum natus XXV mortuus est ve<n=V>en<o=E> quia suas iniurias defendebat Alias sic Cai filiae fecerunt \n
EDCS-09200260.translation: Lucius Alius, son of Caius, aged 25, died. Because he defended his own injuries. His daughters, Alias and Caius, made this.
EDCS-09200260.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Alius, son of Caius, who died at age 25, with his daughters noted as the dedicator.

EDCS-09200261.text_edition: \n Caio Rusio Antiocho viva Zosima liberta fecit\n
EDCS-09200261.translation: To Gaius Rusius Antiochus. Zosima, his freedwoman, made this while he was alive.
EDCS-09200261.summary: A dedication to Gaius Rusius Antiochus, made by his freedwoman during his lifetime.

EDCS-09200263.text_edition: \n Lucius Lucretius Firmus miles ex classe praetoria Miseniensis sic ex liburna Iustitia centuria Cai Minuci Auxiliaris vixit annos \n
EDCS-09200263.translation: Lucius Lucretius Firmus, soldier from the Praetorian fleet of Misenum, from the Liburna, Iustitia, century of Gaius Minucius Auxiliaris, lived [years].
EDCS-09200263.summary: An inscription for Lucius Lucretius Firmus, a soldier from the Misenum fleet, recording his service and partially damaged life details.

EDCS-09200267.summary: A fragmentary inscription, likely the beginning of a dedication or memorial, mentioning Iulius Galenus.

EDCS-09200268.text_edition: \n rior collegi Redemptus immunes Publius Flavius Lucius Terentius Aquilius Marcus A Publius A - M \n
EDCS-09200268.translation: [...] of the college, Redemptus immunes, Publius Flavius Lucius, Terentius Aquilius, Marcus A[...], Publius A[...], Marcus [...]
EDCS-09200268.summary: A very fragmentary inscription possibly related to a college or association, listing several names.

EDCS-09200269.text_edition: \n Lucius Suetius filius Mar tribunus militum cohortis Q \n
EDCS-09200269.translation: Lucius Suetius, son of Mar[cus?], military tribune of the cohort [cohort name?]
EDCS-09200269.summary: A fragmentary inscription mentioning Lucius Suetius, son of Mar[cus?], as a military tribune.

EDCS-09200270.summary: A very brief inscription indicating the burial place of someone named Fulgur.

EDCS-09200271.text_edition: \n Aurelius Luci filius Quintus Flavius Quinti filius IIviri locaverunt idemque probaverunt\n
EDCS-09200271.translation: Aurelius Quintus, son of Lucius, and Flavius Quintus, son of Quintus, as duovirs, contracted [the work] and as such approved it.
EDCS-09200271.summary: This inscription records the officials who contracted and approved a public work, likely from the early imperial period.

EDCS-09200273.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Tiberius Claudius A..., possibly part of a dedication or honorary title.

EDCS-09200280.text_edition: \n Sextia Publi filia Etrusca Iovi Optimo Maximo dono dedit\n
EDCS-09200280.translation: Sextia Etrusca, daughter of Publius, gave [this] as a gift to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-09200280.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Sextia Etrusca to Jupiter, likely from the Roman Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-09200282.text_edition: \n Lucius Tititinus Neo Herculi dat libens merito\n
EDCS-09200282.translation: Lucius Tititinus Neo gladly gave [this] to Hercules as deserved.
EDCS-09200282.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Tititinus Neo to the god Hercules.

EDCS-09200283.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly recording a name and a dedication or funerary context.

EDCS-09300893.text_edition: \n Druso Caesari Tiberi Augusti filio divi Augusti nepoti NI \n
EDCS-09300893.translation: To Drusus Caesar, son of Tiberius Augustus, grandson of the deified Augustus, NI
EDCS-09300893.summary: This inscription is an honorific dedication to Drusus Caesar, son of Tiberius, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-09300898.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ritiniae Cai filiae Iustinae quae vixit annis LIIII mense I diebus X Didi Vitulus et Silvester matri dulcissimae et bene merenti fecerunt\n
EDCS-09300898.translation: To the departed spirits. Ritinia Iustina, daughter of Caius, who lived 54 years, 1 month, and 10 days. Didi Vitulus and Silvester made [this] for their dearest and well-deserving mother.
EDCS-09300898.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ritinia Iustina, set up by her sons Didi Vitulus and Silvester.

EDCS-09300899.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Didio Cai filio Sabatina Vitulo iuveni amantissimo qui vixit nnos XXXV menses X dies XV Iunia Rufina coniugi incomparabili et fili patri bene merenti fecerunt\n
EDCS-09300899.translation: To the departed spirits. For Gaius Didius, son of Caius, Sabatinus Vitulus, the most beloved youth, who lived 35 years, 10 months, and 15 days. Iunia Rufina made [this] for her incomparable husband and well-deserving father.
EDCS-09300899.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Didius, son of Caius, set up by his wife Iunia Rufina.

EDCS-09300900.text_edition: \n Caio Didio Cai filio Sabatina Saturnino equiti Romano qui vixit annis VIIII mensibus XI diebus Caius Didius Vitulus et Sv fecerunt\n
EDCS-09300900.translation: For Gaius Didius, son of Caius, Sabatinus Saturninus, Roman equestrian, who lived 9 years, 11 months, and 10 days. Gaius Didius Vitulus and Sv made [this].
EDCS-09300900.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for the young Gaius Didius Sabatinus Saturninus, an equestrian, set up by his father Gaius Didius Vitulus.

EDCS-09300901.text_edition: \n Varena Cnaei liberta Hilara in agro IX\n
EDCS-09300901.translation: Varena Hilara, freedwoman of Gnaeus, in the district of IX
EDCS-09300901.summary: This inscription records Varena Hilara, a freedwoman, and likely refers to a property or burial location.

EDCS-09300902.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Plotius Sabbo.

EDCS-09300903.text_edition: \n Quintus Iunius Cauhcus sibi et Iuniae Eglogeni collibertae suae\n
EDCS-09300903.translation: Quintus Iunius Cauhcus for himself and for Junia Eglogene, his freedwoman.
EDCS-09300903.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Iunius Cauhcus and his freedwoman Junia Eglogene, likely set up by Cauhcus.

EDCS-09300904.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Cornelius Sisenna.

EDCS-09400318.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quinto Vedio Sereno fecit Vedia Ianuaria mater filio pientissimo vixit annos IIX menses V\n
EDCS-09400318.translation: To the Divine Manes. Vedia Ianuaria made this for her son Quintus Vedius Serenus, most devoted, who lived 8 years, 5 months.
EDCS-09400318.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Vedius Serenus, made by his mother Vedia Ianuaria, recording his age at death.

EDCS-09400319.text_edition: \n Lucio Pomponio Polliano Helicon et Ionis filio dulcissimo sibi et suis\n
EDCS-09400319.translation: To Lucius Pomponius Pollianus, son of Helicon and Ionis, most beloved, for himself and his family.
EDCS-09400319.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Pomponius Pollianus, intended for himself and his family, set up by Helicon and Ionis.

EDCS-09400320.text_edition: \n Valeriae Saturninae coniugi Victor maritus fecit\n
EDCS-09400320.translation: Victor made this for Valeria Saturnina, his wife.
EDCS-09400320.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Saturnina, made by her husband Victor.

EDCS-09401053.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aeli Agrippini qui vixit annis XLVI mensibus II diebus VIIII Aurelia Saturnina coniugi cum quo vixit nnis XVIIII mensibus III bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-09401053.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelia Saturnina made this for her husband Aelius Agrippinus, who lived 46 years, 2 months, and 9 days, for his well-deserved memory, with whom she lived 19 years, 3 months.
EDCS-09401053.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelius Agrippinus, made by his wife Aurelia Saturnina, recording his age and the duration of their marriage.

EDCS-09401055.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Noviae Rufinae parentes et maritus filiae suae bene merenti quae vixit annos XXVIII\n
EDCS-09401055.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Novia Rufina, his daughter, well-deserved, whom her parents and husband survived, who lived 28 years.
EDCS-09401055.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Novia Rufina, made by her parents and husband, recording her age at death.

EDCS-09401056.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Naeviae Victoriae libertae et coniugi rarissimae Maternus bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-09401056.translation: To the Divine Manes. Maternus made this for Naevia Victoria, his freedwoman and wife, most rare, for her well-deserved memory.
EDCS-09401056.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Naevia Victoria, made by Maternus, who identifies her as his freedwoman and wife.

EDCS-09401407.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus pontifex maximus tribuniciae potestatis XIV imperator XI consul III pater patriae et Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus tribuniciae potestatis IX consul II pater patriae Parthicus maximus Brittanicus maximus pontem Apollinis maris et fluminum violentia eversum a fundamentis pecunia sua fecerunt\n
EDCS-09401407.translation: Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, chief priest, in his 14th year of tribunician power, emperor for the 11th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, and Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, in his 9th year of tribunician power, consul for the 2nd time, father of the fatherland, Parthicus Maximus, Brittanicus Maximus, built the bridge of Apollo, destroyed by the violence of the sea and rivers, from its foundations with their own money.
EDCS-09401407.summary: This is an inscription recording the rebuilding of the Bridge of Apollo by Emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla, funded by themselves, likely in the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09401408.text_edition: \n Marcus Galicius Diophantus fecit S III\n
EDCS-09401408.translation: Marcus Galicius Diophantus made this, 3 units.
EDCS-09401408.summary: This is a dedication made by Marcus Galicius Diophantus, possibly of a statue or structure, with an unclear unit of measurement.

EDCS-09401409.text_edition: \n Tiberius Iulius Heraclida signum Silvano libens donum posuit\n
EDCS-09401409.translation: Tiberius Iulius Heraclida willingly placed this statue as a gift to Silvanus.
EDCS-09401409.summary: This inscription records a voluntary offering of a statue to the god Silvanus by Tiberius Iulius Heraclida.

EDCS-09401411.text_edition: \n Hermaes sic aula Faustinae coniugi bene merenti fecit coniux quae vixit mecum annis VIIII mensis sic VI dies IV\n
EDCS-09401411.translation: Hermaes made this for Faustina, his well-deserving wife, with whom he lived for 9 years, 6 months, and 4 days.
EDCS-09401411.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Hermaes for his wife Faustina, detailing the length of their marriage.

EDCS-09401412.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus resiste viator et lege non dignus morti abreptus sum iuvenis annorum XXVIII cui nec bonitas nec forma defuit Flavia Ianuaria fecit Olympo coniugi bene merenti cum quo vixi a prima puaeritiae sic sine iniuria fatis iniquis nati qui tan sic cito disiuncti sumus sit tibi terra l sicaevis\n
EDCS-09401412.translation: Resist, traveler, and read, O spirits of the departed. I was snatched away by death, young, 28 years old, lacking neither goodness nor beauty. Flavia Ianuaria made this for Olympos, her well-deserving husband, with whom I lived from early girlhood without wrongdoing, destined by fate to be separated so quickly; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-09401412.summary: This is a tombstone epitaph set up by Flavia Ianuaria for her deceased husband Olympos, lamenting their premature separation by fate.

EDCS-09401414.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely the title of a monument or ownership mark, referring to Gnaeus Domitius Geminius.

EDCS-09401415.text_edition: \n Sextus Lartius Sexti libertus Phileros sibi et Caeciliae Eutychini coniugi amantissumae et suis fecit\n
EDCS-09401415.translation: Sextus Lartius Phileros, freedman of Sextus, made this for himself, for his most loving wife Caecilia Eutychis, and for his family.
EDCS-09401415.summary: This funerary inscription records Sextus Lartius Phileros dedicating a monument for himself, his wife, and his family.

EDCS-09401416.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Caio Messio Quinto Traiano Decius sic Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli III patri patriae restitutori sacrorum et libertatis res publica Cosanorum dicata numini maiestatiquei sic aeius sic\n
EDCS-09401416.translation: The Republic of the people of Cosanum dedicated this to the divine majesty of the Emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, consul for the third time, father of the country, restorer of the sacred rites and liberty.
EDCS-09401416.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by the community of Cosanum to Emperor Trajan Decius, highlighting his titles and accomplishments.

EDCS-09700128.text_edition: \n Quinto Volusio Luci filio Luci nepoti Saturnino consuli sodali Augustali sodali Titio fratri Arvali legato Caesaris at sic census accipiendos provinciae Belgicae\n
EDCS-09700128.translation: To Quintus Volusius Saturninus, son of Lucius, grandson of Lucius, consul, Augustalian fellow priest, Titian fellow priest, brother, legate of Caesar for receiving the census of the province of Belgica.
EDCS-09700128.summary: This is a dedication to Quintus Volusius Saturninus, listing his various high-ranking political, religious, and military offices, including his role in census collection.

EDCS-09700131.text_edition: \n Lucio Allio Luci filio Stellatina Volusiano tribuno laticlavio legionis XII fulminatae certae constantis quaestori provinciae Baeticae aedili Ceriali collegium fabrum et centonariorum patrono optimo\n
EDCS-09700131.translation: To Lucius Allius Stellanus Volusianus, son of Lucius, of the tribe Stellatina, military tribune of the 12th Legion Fulminata, Constant and True, quaestor of the province of Baetica, aedile, as the best patron of the college of smiths and weavers.
EDCS-09700131.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Allius Stellanus Volusianus, detailing his military and administrative career and his role as patron of a collegium.

EDCS-09700132.text_edition: \n Isi et Serapi Caesennia Luci filia Prisca Vitellia Spuri filia Ingenua Caesenni Prisci sua pecunia posuerunt\n
EDCS-09700132.translation: Isis and Serapis. Caesennia Prisca, daughter of Lucius, of the tribe Vitellia, and Ingenua, daughter of Spurius Caesennius Priscus, placed these at their own expense.
EDCS-09700132.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to the gods Isis and Serapis by two women, Caesennia Prisca and Ingenua, funded by themselves.

EDCS-09700133.text_edition: \n Publius Luci filius Rufus Titus Lucilius filius Priscus IIIIviri []\n
EDCS-09700133.translation: Publius Lucius filius Rufus, Titus Lucilius filius Priscus, IIIIviri.
EDCS-09700133.summary: This inscription lists four officials (IIIIviri) from the time of the Roman Republic or early Empire.
