EDCS-22100205.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Laelius Iucundus, son of Gaius, and for Laelius Paullinus, his most devoted brother. He lived 55 years. Caius Disinius Verus for his deserving friend.
EDCS-22100205.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by several individuals for Gaius Laelius Iucundus and his brother, including a friend and possibly relatives, providing his age at death.

EDCS-22100206.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Laelia Restituta for Gaius Laelius Pius.
EDCS-22100206.summary: A funerary monument erected by Laelia Restituta for Gaius Laelius Pius.

EDCS-22100208.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Larius Clemens, son of Lucius, of the Tromentina tribe. He lived 2 years, 10 months, and 1 day. Matteia Olumphias for her dearest grandson.
EDCS-22100208.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Larius Clemens, a young child, erected by his grandmother, Matteia Olumphias.

EDCS-22100213.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Publius Magius Verus. Lucius Magius, Verona, and Publius Magius Iustus, his sons, and Pupa, his freedwoman, took care to have this made for the well-deserving deceased.
EDCS-22100213.summary: A funerary monument erected by the sons and a freedwoman for Publius Magius Verus, indicating his deserving character.

EDCS-22100214.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Moschidis made this for Agathemer, the freedwoman and dearest companion of Titus Sulcanus Hermas.
EDCS-22100214.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Moschidis for his companion Agathemer.

EDCS-22100225.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Proculeius Fidus, Titus Vestinius Venus[t]us, and Vestinia Sabina made this for their most dutiful son, who lived 18 years, 8 months, and 12 days.
EDCS-22100225.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Proculeius Fidus, detailing his age at death and dedicated by his parents.

EDCS-22100226.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aulus Publicius Iustus and Citronia Gemellina made this for their most beloved and deserving son, Publicius Iustus, who lived 19 years, 7 months, and 16 days, their most dutiful son.
EDCS-22100226.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publicius Iustus, detailing his age at death and dedicated by his parents.

EDCS-22100227.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Cl[---] Romani lies here, a sweet freedman without his fault.
EDCS-22100227.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a freedman named Romani.

EDCS-22100232.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Silius Sabinus made this for his best and most dutiful brother, Lucius Silius Severus.
EDCS-22100232.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Gaius Silius Sabinus for his brother Lucius Silius Severus.

EDCS-22100237.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Versenus Prudens made this for his son, Lucius Versenus Optimus, who lived 14 years, 5 days, and 3 hours.
EDCS-22100237.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Lucius Versenus Prudens for his son Lucius Versenus Optimus, detailing his age at death.

EDCS-22100238.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Volcacius M(arci) l(ibertus) F[---] and Volcacia Amabilis, parents, made this for their sweetest son, Marcus Volcacus Probu[s].
EDCS-22100238.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by parents for their son.

EDCS-22100240.translation: Lucius Volumnius Perusinus for himself and [---]
EDCS-22100240.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication for the self and possibly others, but is incomplete.

EDCS-22100246.translation: For the health of our children Gaius and Titus Pomponius. Sacred to Aesculapius and Hygieia. Ephaestas, a freedwoman, willingly fulfilled her vow as was deserved.
EDCS-22100246.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Aesculapius and Hygieia for the health of children.

EDCS-22100247.translation: For the health of our Quintus and Triaria and their children. Sacred to Aesculapius.
EDCS-22100247.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Aesculapius for the health of a family.

EDCS-22100248.translation: Sacred to Apollo, for the health of our Triaria.
EDCS-22100248.summary: This is a dedication to Apollo, likely from a private individual or group, concerning the health of a woman named Triaria.

EDCS-22100249.translation: Vettius Primus willingly gave this gift to the Ambrosial Gods.
EDCS-22100249.summary: This is a votive offering made by Vettius Primus to the Ambrosial Gods.

EDCS-22100250.translation: Gavia Statuta willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Lari Victor.
EDCS-22100250.summary: This inscription records Gavia Statuta fulfilling a vow to Lari Victor.

EDCS-22100252.translation: The council [of the city] to the Emperor Caesar L. Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus, chief priest, in his second tribunician power, acclaimed imperator four times, consul twice, proconsul, father of the country...
EDCS-22100252.summary: This is a dedication from a city council to Emperor Septimius Severus, listing his titles and offices, likely from his second consulship (around 193-211 AD).

EDCS-22100255.translation: [Flavius Titi]anus, a most distinguished man, curator of the republic, devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-22100255.summary: This inscription records the devotion of Flavius Titianus, a high-ranking official, to the divine majesty of an emperor.

EDCS-22100259.translation: To Marcus Agrippa, son of Lucius, consul.
EDCS-22100259.summary: This is a dedication or memorial to Marcus Agrippa, son of Lucius, who held the office of consul.

EDCS-22100260.translation: To Marcus Fabius Magnus Valerianus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, member of the board of fifteen for judging legal disputes, military tribune with broad stripe of the Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis, member of the board of fifteen for performing sacred rites, quaestor by candidacy, member of the board of six, leader of turma V, tribune of the plebs, praetor, Lupercus, curator of the public affairs of Velitrae, curator of the Via Latina, juridical official of the region of Tuscia and Picenum, legate of the Augusti of the Legio I Italica. The most splendid council of the Clusini, to their most excellent patron, for his benevolence towards them.
EDCS-22100260.summary: This lengthy inscription honors Marcus Fabius Magnus Valerianus, detailing his extensive career in various military, political, and administrative roles, and his patronage of the city of Clusium.

EDCS-22100263.translation: To Aulus Luc[---], of the Arnensian tribe, [centurion] primipilus of the [---] legion, [and] of the Legio X [---]
EDCS-22100263.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Aulus Luc[---], a primipilus (chief centurion) from the Arnensian tribe, who served in at least two legions, one of which was the Legio X.

EDCS-22100264.translation: Lucius Petillius [---], soldier of the [---] cohort of the praetorian guard, served [---] years.
EDCS-22100264.summary: This inscription records information about Lucius Petillius, a soldier in the Praetorian Guard, including his service length.

EDCS-22100265.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Vesi Verus, soldier of the V Praetorian cohort, lived 21 years, 7 months, and 9 days. Octavia Threpte, his most devoted mother.
EDCS-22100265.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lucius Vesi Verus, a soldier in the V Praetorian cohort, who died at the age of 21, erected by his mother.

EDCS-22100269.translation: To Honor Lucius Tiberius Maefanatius Basilius, a distinguished man, formerly one of the fifteen governors of the provinces, defender of the town council and of the citizens of the eternal city, because he governed his citizens and the people of Clusium with integrity, loved them with affection, supported them with generosity, and nurtured them with humanity, for his distinguished services, the entire council, with joyful acclamations and every wish, offered him a statue.
EDCS-22100269.summary: This inscription records a statue erected by the council of Clusium to honor Lucius Tiberius Maefanatius Basilius for his good governance and public service.

EDCS-22100270.translation: To Lucius Alfius Quietus, twice chief magistrate, quinquennial priest of Augustus, prefect of the craftsmen, twice aedile of Etruria, Lucius Alfius Florus, his patron, (erected this) for his well-deserving father and for himself.
EDCS-22100270.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Lucius Alfius Florus for his father, Lucius Alfius Quietus, who held several important magistracies, and for himself.

EDCS-22100271.translation: Gaius Considius, son of Gaius, grandson of Lucius, aquaduct commissioner, born in Cominia.
EDCS-22100271.summary: This is a personal inscription identifying Gaius Considius, son of Gaius, grandson of Lucius, an aqueduct commissioner, and stating his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-22100272.translation: To Lucius Fonteius Justus, quaestor, aedile, duovir.
EDCS-22100272.summary: This inscription lists the magistracies held by Lucius Fonteius Justus, including quaestor, aedile, and duovir.

EDCS-22100273.translation: To Quintus Gellius Villianus, duovir, advocate of the people, the council (erected this) for his constant services to this state.
EDCS-22100273.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the town council to Quintus Gellius Villianus, a duovir and advocate, for his services to the state.

EDCS-22100275.translation: To Rubria Polla, daughter of Lucius, mother, for herself and for Quintus Antonius Marusai, son of Gaius, of the Arnensian tribe, aedile, duovir, quinquennial magistrate.
EDCS-22100275.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Rubria Polla for herself and for Quintus Antonius Marusai, who served as aedile, duovir, and quinquennial magistrate.

EDCS-22100276.text_edition: \n Gellius Cai filius AVnus Tutilius Auli filius Capito IIIIviri iure dicundo porticus ex senatus consulto sua pecunia refecerunt\n
EDCS-22100276.translation: Gellius Avnus, son of Caius, and Tutilius Capito, son of Aulus, four-year magistrates, rebuilt the porticoes with their own money by decree of the senate.
EDCS-22100276.summary: This inscription records the rebuilding of porticoes by two four-year magistrates, Gellius Avnus and Tutilius Capito, using their own funds, as decreed by the senate.

EDCS-22100279.translation: To Lucius Volusius Philoerotis, freedman of Lucius, aedile.
EDCS-22100279.summary: This is a personal inscription identifying Lucius Volusius Philoerotis, a freedman, and noting his position as aedile.

EDCS-22100283.translation: To Gaius [3]aenius [3]iis[3]inus Oseius [3] Ples, a public place was granted by decree of the decurions for his services.
EDCS-22100283.summary: This inscription records the granting of a public space to Gaius [3]aenius for his merits, as decided by the decurions.

EDCS-22100287.translation: To the gods, the departed. To Velox, the excellent silversmith, [his] son[s?].
EDCS-22100287.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Velox, an excellent silversmith, likely erected by his sons.

EDCS-22100288.translation: Gaius Acilius, son of Lucius, of the Tribonian tribe, architect.
EDCS-22100288.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Acilius, who was an architect.

EDCS-22100290.translation: Publius Vettius Antiochus, freedman of Decimus, a purple-dyer.
EDCS-22100290.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Vettius Antiochus, a purple-dyer.

EDCS-22100291.summary: This is a very short inscription, likely a name on a monument or tomb.

EDCS-22100292.translation: Lucius Papirius, son of Aulus, of the Arnensian tribe.
EDCS-22100292.summary: This inscription records the name and tribal affiliation of Lucius Papirius.

EDCS-22100293.translation: Aulus Papirius, son of Lucius, of the Alfia tribe, aged 43 years.
EDCS-22100293.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aulus Papirius, who died at the age of 43.

EDCS-22100294.translation: Aulus Papirius, [son of ---]nonis, of the Alfia tribe, aged 43 years.
EDCS-22100294.summary: This is a damaged funerary epitaph for Aulus Papirius, likely recording his age and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-22100295.translation: Of Sextus Papirius Marcus, son of Sextus.
EDCS-22100295.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication or ownership by Sextus Papirius Marcus.

EDCS-22100296.translation: Aulus Papirius, son of Aulus, of the Satellia tribe.
EDCS-22100296.summary: This inscription records the name and tribal affiliation of Aulus Papirius.

EDCS-22100297.translation: To Sexius Papirius of the Aurunci.
EDCS-22100297.summary: This inscription is likely a dedication to Sexius Papirius.

EDCS-22100298.translation: Aulus Papirius, son of Aulus, Fenestella.
EDCS-22100298.summary: This inscription records the name of Aulus Papirius and possibly his cognomen.

EDCS-22100299.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of a woman, Satellia Velizza.

EDCS-22100324.translation: Arria, daughter of Gaius, to Arisna Titilnia.
EDCS-22100324.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or memorial from Arria, daughter of Gaius, to Arisna Titilnia.

EDCS-22100325.translation: Aulus Rustius Gallus, son of Aulus, born of Murrenia.
EDCS-22100325.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of a Roman man, Aulus Rustius Gallus.

EDCS-22100326.translation: Gaius Papirius Maxillo, son of Lucius.
EDCS-22100326.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of a Roman man, Gaius Papirius Maxillo.

EDCS-22100327.translation: Lucius Papirius Pamphilus, freedman of Gnaeus.
EDCS-22100327.summary: This inscription records the name of a freedman, Lucius Papirius Pamphilus.

EDCS-22100328.translation: Thannia, daughter of Sextus, of the Trebonia family.
EDCS-22100328.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of a woman, Thannia, from the Trebonia family.

EDCS-22100329.summary: This inscription records the name of an individual, Thanna Naeipurs.

EDCS-22100332.translation: Of Lucius Arrius, son of Arruno.
EDCS-22100332.summary: This inscription appears to be a genitive case, possibly indicating ownership or relation, for Lucius Arrius, son of Arruno.

EDCS-22100333.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of a woman, Blaesia.

EDCS-22100334.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of a woman, Larthia Herennia.

EDCS-22100335.translation: Gaius Latinius, son of Gaius, called Ladile.
EDCS-22100335.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Gaius Latinius Ladile, son of Gaius.

EDCS-22100336.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Publius Marcius Bucchio.

EDCS-22100340.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Larthia Marina, daughter of Caina.

EDCS-22100341.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Lartia Marina, daughter of Lartius.

EDCS-22100342.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Murrenia Veliza.

EDCS-22100349.translation: Lucius Pontius Rufus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-22100349.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Lucius Pontius Rufus, son of Lucius.

EDCS-22100350.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Pontia Salvia, freedwoman of Lucius.

EDCS-22100351.translation: Gaius Vensius, son of Gaius, called Gaius, or Venzile Alfnalisle.
EDCS-22100351.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Gaius Vensius, son of Gaius, possibly with alternative names or a difficult reading.

EDCS-22100352.translation: Gaius Vensius, son of Gaius, born into the Caesia family.
EDCS-22100352.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Gaius Vensius, son of Gaius, noting his birth into the Caesia family.

EDCS-22100353.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Petillius Pavo.

EDCS-22100354.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Lucius Sertorius, likely from a funerary monument.

EDCS-22100355.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Volchacia, likely from a funerary monument.

EDCS-22100364.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Aulus Fabius Iucnus, possibly on a monument or altar.

EDCS-22100366.translation: Lucius Phisius Buco, freedman of Lucius.
EDCS-22100366.summary: This inscription records the name and manumission of Lucius Phisius Buco, likely from a funerary monument.

EDCS-22100367.translation: Lucius Acilius, son of Lucius, born of Eromacae.
EDCS-22100367.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Lucius Acilius, mentioning his mother Eromacae, likely from a funerary monument.

EDCS-22100368.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Calpurnia Paula, likely from a funerary monument.

EDCS-22100369.translation: Quintus Granius Proculus, son of Sextus, born of Calpurnia.
EDCS-22100369.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Quintus Granius Proculus, mentioning his mother Calpurnia, likely from a funerary monument.

EDCS-22100370.translation: Sextus Granius Hispanus, son of Sextus.
EDCS-22100370.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Sextus Granius Hispanus, likely from a funerary monument.

EDCS-22100371.translation: Gaius Sentius, son of Gaius, born of Grania Hannossa.
EDCS-22100371.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Gaius Sentius, mentioning his mother Grania Hannossa, likely from a funerary monument.

EDCS-22100372.translation: Quintus Titius Srablo, son of Gaius, born of Coelia.
EDCS-22100372.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Quintus Titius Srablo, mentioning his mother Coelia, likely from a funerary monument.

EDCS-22100373.translation: Gaius Titius, son of Gaius, born into the Larcia tribe, from Srablio.
EDCS-22100373.summary: This is a personal name inscription, likely from a tombstone or funerary monument, identifying Gaius Titius and his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-22100374.translation: Gaius Titius, son of Lucius, Pupillus; born into the Arria tribe.
EDCS-22100374.summary: This is a personal name inscription, likely from a tombstone or funerary monument, identifying Gaius Titius Pupillus and his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-22100375.translation: Gaius Titius Pupillus, son of Lucius, born into the Arria tribe.
EDCS-22100375.summary: This is a personal name inscription, likely from a tombstone or funerary monument, identifying Gaius Titius Pupillus and his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-22100376.summary: This is a personal name inscription, likely from a tombstone or funerary monument, identifying Titia Vesconia.

EDCS-22100377.summary: This is a personal name inscription, likely from a tombstone or funerary monument, identifying Titia Vesconia, with a slight variation in spelling of the cognomen.

EDCS-22100378.summary: This is a personal name inscription, likely from a tombstone or funerary monument, identifying Helvia and her father Lucius.

EDCS-22100379.translation: Caia Secunda Titia, daughter of Titus, Vesconia.
EDCS-22100379.summary: This is a personal name inscription, likely from a tombstone or funerary monument, identifying Caia Secunda Titia Vesconia and her father Titus.

EDCS-22100381.translation: Quintus Scribonius, son of Gaius, (a) respected citizen.
EDCS-22100381.summary: This is a personal name inscription, likely from a tombstone or funerary monument, identifying Quintus Scribonius and noting his status as a respected citizen.

EDCS-22100382.summary: This is a personal name inscription, likely from a tombstone or funerary monument, identifying Aulus Scribonius and his father Gaius.

EDCS-22100391.translation: Quintus Haerinna, son of Quintus, born into the Sentia tribe.
EDCS-22100391.summary: This is a personal name inscription, likely from a tombstone or funerary monument, identifying Quintus Haerinna and his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-22100398.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Pisentius, identified by his mother Mavia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22100399.translation: Gaius Isentius, son of Gaius, born of Varia.
EDCS-22100399.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Isentius, identified by his father Gaius and his mother Varia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22100400.translation: Albanius, son of Gaius, descendant of Lucius Pisentius.
EDCS-22100400.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Albanius, son of Gaius and descendant of Lucius Pisentius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22100401.summary: This is a dedication or funerary inscription honoring Lucius Sartage, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22100402.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Sartagus, identified by his father Lucius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22100404.translation: To Aulus Scandilius, son of Aulus, born of Caesia.
EDCS-22100404.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aulus Scandilius, identified by his father Aulus and mother Caesia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22100405.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly funerary or dedicatory, mentioning Larth Scansa and a patronymic or gentile name, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22100407.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Varia, identified by her father Aulus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22100410.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Hastia Veratronia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22100411.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Thania Vipinia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22100417.translation: Gaius Gellius Crassus, born to Annia.
EDCS-22100417.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Gellius Crassus, identifying his mother Annia.

EDCS-22100418.translation: Gaius Gellius Crassus, son of Gaius, of the Arnensis tribe, born to Murtia.
EDCS-22100418.summary: This funerary epitaph identifies Gaius Gellius Crassus, his father Gaius, his mother Murtia, and his tribal affiliation (Arnensis).

EDCS-22100419.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Aelius Carinus.

EDCS-22100420.summary: This fragmentary funerary epitaph likely records the name of the deceased.

EDCS-22100421.translation: To Lucius Aelius Eutelpistus, his grandfather, Aulus Caedicius Pompeianus, well-deserving.
EDCS-22100421.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Aulus Caedicius Pompeianus to his grandfather, Lucius Aelius Eutelpistus.

EDCS-22100422.translation: To Emilia Fabianena, his most beloved daughter, [---]. Aemilius Ermeas and Flavia Iustina, parents, set this up. She lived 22 years, 3 months, 16 days.
EDCS-22100422.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by parents Aemilius Ermeas and Flavia Iustina for their daughter Emilia Fabianena, who died at age 22.

EDCS-22100423.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aemilia Paulina, a most unfortunate girl, who lived twelve years. Telesphoris and Au[---]nus, brothers, made [this].
EDCS-22100423.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to the spirits of the underworld for Aemilia Paulina, a girl who died at age twelve, and was made by her brothers.

EDCS-22100424.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Agathemerus, a father, from his son, well-deserving.
EDCS-22100424.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to the spirits of the underworld for a father named Agathemerus, by his son.

EDCS-22100425.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Lucius Alphius and his father Aulus.

EDCS-22100426.translation: C(aius) Alfius, son of Aulus, born to Cainnia.
EDCS-22100426.summary: This funerary epitaph identifies Gaius Alfius, his father Aulus, and his mother Cainnia.

EDCS-22100427.translation: Quintus Alfius Optatus to his most dutiful parents, Alfia Telidis, surviving them.
EDCS-22100427.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a son for his deceased parents.

EDCS-22100428.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Alfia Hospita.

EDCS-22100429.translation: To Alfia Prima, freedwoman of Quintus.
EDCS-22100429.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a freedwoman named Alfia Prima.

EDCS-22100430.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Alfia Secunda.

EDCS-22100431.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a freedwoman named Amatia Salvia.

EDCS-22100432.translation: Amethystus, slave of Titus Alfius Hilarius.
EDCS-22100432.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a slave named Amethystus.

EDCS-22100433.translation: Aulus Ancarius Tolmaca, son of Aulus, born.
EDCS-22100433.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aulus Ancarius Tolmaca, indicating his birth and parentage.

EDCS-22100434.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tahnia Anainia, daughter of Cominius.

EDCS-22100437.translation: Gaius Annius, son of Gaius, of the Arnensis tribe.
EDCS-22100437.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Annius, son of Gaius, indicating his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-22100438.translation: Gaius Annius, son of Lucius, of the Coelia gens.
EDCS-22100438.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Annius, son of Lucius, identifying his family and lineage.

EDCS-22100440.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Anneia, daughter of Gaius, Parten[---], Gaius Clodius Privatus, son of Lucius, her well-deserving one.
EDCS-22100440.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Anneia by Gaius Clodius Privatus, likely her husband or relative.

EDCS-22100441.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Anniania Maximilla by her husband.

EDCS-22100443.translation: Anicia Iucunda, freedwoman of the woman Anicia.
EDCS-22100443.summary: This inscription records the name and status of Anicia Iucunda, a freedwoman.

EDCS-22100444.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Anicia Maior.

EDCS-22100445.summary: This inscription records the name and status of Appuleia Citheris, a freedwoman.

EDCS-22100450.summary: This inscription simply records the name Gaius Arrius, possibly on a tombstone or property marker.

EDCS-22100452.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Lucius Arrius Fortunatus. Lucius Arrius Profuturus, his son.
EDCS-22100452.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Arrius Profuturus to his son, Lucius Arrius Fortunatus.

EDCS-22100454.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Arria Compses. Staphis and Gorge, to their mother.
EDCS-22100454.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Staphis and Gorge to their mother, Arria Compses.

EDCS-22100455.translation: Arria Philematio(?), freedwoman of the woman.
EDCS-22100455.summary: This inscription records the name and status of Arria Philematio(?), a freedwoman.

EDCS-22100461.translation: Gaius Babius Haruspex, son of Gaius.
EDCS-22100461.summary: This inscription records the name, filiation, and profession (Haruspex - diviner) of Gaius Babius.

EDCS-22100462.translation: Gaius Baebius, son of Lucius, a haruspex.
EDCS-22100462.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Baebius, identified by his name, filiation, and profession as a haruspex.

EDCS-22100463.translation: To I[---] T[---], from Philuthymus, the assistant.
EDCS-22100463.summary: This is a dedication to unknown deities or individuals by Philuthymus, who is identified as a vicarius (assistant or substitute).

EDCS-22100466.summary: This inscription records Gaius Caetennius, noting his mother Vesinnia, likely as an epitaph or identification marker.

EDCS-22100468.translation: To the Campanae; Lucius Anius Carpus for himself and for Lucius, our Lucius Carocinus.
EDCS-22100468.summary: This is a dedication to the Campanae, made by Lucius Anius Carpus for himself and for Lucius Carocinus, possibly his patron or relative.

EDCS-22100469.translation: Gaius Gavius, son of Lucius, his son.
EDCS-22100469.summary: This inscription identifies Gaius Gavius, noting his father Lucius and also stating he is a son, likely an epitaph or familial record.

EDCS-22100471.translation: Lucius Cartilius, son of Lucius, a haruspex.
EDCS-22100471.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Cartilius, identified by his name, filiation, and profession as a haruspex.

EDCS-22100474.translation: Tutilia, daughter of Lucius, made this for her husband, Gaius Catinus Secundus.
EDCS-22100474.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Tutilia for her husband, Gaius Catinus Secundus.

EDCS-22100475.translation: Celadus, servant of Turiaes and Faustaes. Lucius Rufrius [...].
EDCS-22100475.summary: This inscription seems to be part of a funerary monument, mentioning slaves and a possible owner or dedicant.

EDCS-22100478.translation: To the departed spirits. Claudius Victor, for his freedwoman and wife, Claudia Eufraxia, who deserved well.
EDCS-22100478.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Claudia Eufraxia by Claudius Victor, her former master and husband, for her well-deserving nature.

EDCS-22100480.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph or name tag for Coelia Anticlia.

EDCS-22100481.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Coelia Peregrina, wife of Epaphroditus son of Coelius Hormus, who deserved well. She lived 25 years.
EDCS-22100481.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Coelia Peregrina, set up by her husband.

EDCS-22100482.translation: To Lucius Consilius Atticus, who lived 20 years.
EDCS-22100482.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Consilius Atticus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-22100483.translation: Lucius Consius Rufus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-22100483.summary: This is a tombstone identification for Lucius Consius Rufus, son of Lucius.

EDCS-22100484.translation: Cornelius Celer, brother, and Cornelia, mother, set up (this monument) for Cornelia Primilla and Cornelia Tertulla.
EDCS-22100484.summary: This funerary monument was erected by a brother and mother for Cornelia Primilla and Cornelia Tertulla.

EDCS-22100486.translation: To Crasinius Florus, a well-deserving husband, (this monument was set up) by Crasinia Ruscinia.
EDCS-22100486.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Crasinius Florus, dedicated by his wife.

EDCS-22100488.translation: Ennia Prima, freedwoman of the woman (owner), is laid here.
EDCS-22100488.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ennia Prima, identifying her status as a freedwoman.

EDCS-22100491.translation: To Fannia Balbilla, daughter of Lucius, an excellent mistress.
EDCS-22100491.summary: This inscription honors Fannia Balbilla, identified as an excellent mistress, likely a funerary dedication.

EDCS-22100493.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Fonteia Septima, mother, (this monument was set up) by her son Fonteius Dionysius.
EDCS-22100493.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fonteia Septima, set up by her son.

EDCS-22100494.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Gavia Victorina, a well-deserving wife, (this monument was set up) by Fonteius Dionysius.
EDCS-22100494.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gavia Victorina, set up by her husband, Fonteius Dionysius.

EDCS-22100495.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Fonteius Victorinus lived 22 years, 8 months, 9 days. Fonteius Dionysius (set this up) for his dearest son. He was here, the first progenitor, so that I might be glorious, if indeed Fortune had not been unfavorable by fate.
EDCS-22100495.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Lucius Fonteius Victorinus and expresses a father's lament for his son's unfulfilled potential due to fate.

EDCS-22100496.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Fonteius Dionysius, deeply loving and forever missing his dearest wife, Gellius Nice, erected this monument.
EDCS-22100496.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his deceased wife.

EDCS-22100498.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. The father, with tears, placed this for his most cherished and dearest daughter, Fonteia Agathetyscheni, who lived 27 years, 7 months, [erected by] her father and mother.
EDCS-22100498.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young woman, possibly set up by her parents, including details of her age at death.

EDCS-22100499.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gavius Glareus set this up for his well-deserving father, Lucius Gavius Moderatus.
EDCS-22100499.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a son for his deceased father.

EDCS-22100500.translation: To the dearest, well-deserving Gellias Thaidis, daughter of..., who lived 27 years, 4 months, 7 days, [and ?] hours. Her most devoted parents, Munatia Caliste and Rufus, set this up.
EDCS-22100500.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young woman, recording her age at death and set up by her parents.

EDCS-22100501.translation: Caius Gavius, son of Quintus, set this up as heir.
EDCS-22100501.summary: An epitaph or dedication by Caius Gavius, indicating he is the heir.

EDCS-22100502.translation: Lucius Gavius, son of Sextus, born in the Volcacian tribe.
EDCS-22100502.summary: A simple inscription identifying Lucius Gavius, son of Sextus, and his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-22100503.translation: Of Lucius Gavius, son of Lucius, [named] Clemens.
EDCS-22100503.summary: An inscription recording the name and filiation of Lucius Gavius Clemens.

EDCS-22100504.summary: An inscription identifying Thannia Gavia and her father.

EDCS-22100506.translation: Gavia Primigenia, freedwoman of Caius Gavi.
EDCS-22100506.summary: An inscription identifying Gavia Primigenia and her former master.

EDCS-22100507.summary: An inscription identifying Gavia Tarntia and her former master.

EDCS-22100509.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gellius Anthianus...
EDCS-22100509.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gellius Anthianus, with the dedicator and specific relationship missing.

EDCS-22100511.translation: Fortunatus, freedman of Sextus Granius, haruspex.
EDCS-22100511.summary: This inscription identifies Fortunatus as the freedman of Sextus Granius and notes his profession as a haruspex (diviner).

EDCS-22100512.translation: Sextus Granius Capito, son of Sextus.
EDCS-22100512.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Sextus Granius Capito.

EDCS-22100513.translation: Sextus Granius Ferox, son of Sextus, of the Arnensis tribe.
EDCS-22100513.summary: This inscription records the name, filiation, and tribal affiliation of Sextus Granius Ferox.

EDCS-22100514.summary: This inscription records the name and parentage of Salassa.

EDCS-22100516.translation: Of Gaius Herennius, son of Quintus.
EDCS-22100516.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Gaius Herennius.

EDCS-22100518.translation: Lucius Hirrius, son of Lucius, born in Udesia.
EDCS-22100518.summary: This inscription records the name, filiation, and place of birth of Lucius Hirrius.

EDCS-22100519.text_edition: \n Gaius Iulius Gai libertus Hieronimus Iulia Selenio soror dedit\n
EDCS-22100519.translation: Gaius Julius Hieronimus, freedman of Gaius, dedicated [this] to his sister Iulia Selenio.
EDCS-22100519.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Gaius Julius Hieronimus to his sister Iulia Selenio.

EDCS-22100521.translation: Gaius Laelius Creticus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-22100521.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Gaius Laelius Creticus.

EDCS-22100524.translation: Lucius Livius Classicus, son of Lucius, of the Arnensis tribe, in front [of the property] 12 feet, in the land 10 feet.
EDCS-22100524.summary: This inscription records the name, filiation, and tribal affiliation of Lucius Livius Classicus, likely related to a land boundary or property marker.

EDCS-22100526.summary: This inscription appears to be the beginning of a dedication or ownership marker, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22100528.translation: Aulus Marcius, son of Publius, and Secunda, daughter of Gaius Seius
EDCS-22100528.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the names of two individuals, likely a husband and wife, from the Roman Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-22100530.translation: Marcia, daughter of Aulus, Stenia, her mother
EDCS-22100530.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying a woman and her mother, likely from the Roman Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-22100531.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Marcius Agathinus lived for 4 years and 10 months. Lucius Marcius Pylades and Claudia Hygia [erected this] for their son.
EDCS-22100531.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young boy, Lucius Marcius Agathinus, with his parents identified as the dedicator, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22100532.translation: Caius Mestlus made [this] for his son L. [---], who lived 26 years.
EDCS-22100532.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a son who lived 26 years, with his father Caius Mestlus identified as the dedicator, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22100534.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of an individual, likely from the Roman Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-22100535.translation: Caius Odies, son of Caius, and Lartia, his wife
EDCS-22100535.summary: This inscription records the names of a couple, Caius Odies and his wife Lartia, likely from the Roman Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-22100539.translation: To the departed spirits. For Lucius Paccus, an excellent friend. Pupia Lactus [erected this].
EDCS-22100539.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Paccus, dedicated by his friend Pupia Lactus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22100541.translation: Sextus Papirius, freedman of Aulus, [dedicated this] to Sextius
EDCS-22100541.summary: This inscription records a dedication from Sextus Papirius, a freedman, to Sextius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22100542.translation: Of Lucius Passienus Apollonius, freedman of Lucius, [and] of Passiena, freedwoman of Lucius, [and] of Philomation
EDCS-22100542.summary: This inscription lists three individuals, likely freedpeople and their associate, possibly associated with a family or property, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22100544.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Pernia Alcimus erected this for his well-deserving Pernia Storge, his daughter.
EDCS-22100544.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a father for his daughter, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-22100546.translation: Marcus Pescennius, born in the territory of Messia, son of Marcus, lived 21 years.
EDCS-22100546.summary: An epitaph for Marcus Pescennius, recording his birth origin and age at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-22100548.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iusta erected this for her father, Sextus Petilius Primigenius.
EDCS-22100548.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a daughter for her father, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-22100725.translation: Gaius Pulfennius, son of Gaius, born in the territory of Pisentia.
EDCS-22100725.summary: An epitaph recording the name, parentage, and birth origin of Gaius Pulfennius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-22100726.translation: Gaius Pulfennius, son of Gaius, called Calamus.
EDCS-22100726.summary: An epitaph recording the name and cognomen of Gaius Pulfennius Calamus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-22100742.translation: Lucius Saenius Saturninus and Isi Sannutia Philete erected this for themselves while alive.
EDCS-22100742.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a couple for themselves during their lifetime, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-22100755.summary: A dedication, possibly funerary, to Lucius Sentius Phoebus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-22100758.summary: An ownership or funerary inscription for Lucius Sentius Nasonis Scariphus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-22100760.summary: An inscription recording the name and parentage of Sentia, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-22100762.summary: An inscription recording the names of two women, Antigona and Sentia Paperis, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-22100763.translation: To the departed spirits. Sentia Prote had this made for her mother Sentia Balbilla.
EDCS-22100763.summary: A funerary dedication by a daughter for her mother.

EDCS-22100767.translation: Sextus Sertorius Sartages, son of Lucius.
EDCS-22100767.summary: An owner's name, likely on a funerary monument or property.

EDCS-22100769.summary: A woman's name and filiation, likely from a funerary context.

EDCS-22100773.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Sentius Aulax made this for his most devoted wife Sthenia Veneria, with whom he lived in marriage for 5 years, 6 months, and 4 days; she lived 27 years. She is buried here, seven paces from Sentius Aulax. Veneria. Here are two sisters, Aphrodite and Veneria.
EDCS-22100773.summary: A funerary monument for a wife, specifying the duration of her marriage and life, and mentioning other family members buried nearby.

EDCS-22100776.translation: To the departed spirits. Arria Theoris [made this] for her well-deserving Lucius Tarquitius Verecundus and for herself.
EDCS-22100776.summary: A funerary dedication by a wife for her husband, also for herself, indicating a joint tomb.

EDCS-22100777.translation: To the departed spirits. Tertullinus made this for his most beloved wife Adiecta, well-deserving.
EDCS-22100777.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband for his wife.

EDCS-22100786.summary: An ownership inscription, possibly for a tomb or property.

EDCS-22100788.summary: A woman's name and manumission details, likely from a funerary context.

EDCS-22100789.translation: To the departed spirits. Titia Crescentina [made this] for her husband Titienus Secularis.
EDCS-22100789.summary: A funerary dedication by a wife for her husband.

EDCS-22100793.translation: Quintus Trebonius, son of Quintus, son [of Quintus].
EDCS-22100793.summary: A name and extended filiation, possibly for a funerary monument or public record.

EDCS-22100794.translation: Quintus Trebonius Cacecinia, son of Gaius, by birth.
EDCS-22100794.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Quintus Trebonius Cacecinia by his given name, father's name, and tribal name.

EDCS-22100795.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Trebonius Donatus. Lucius Trebonius Spinther for his brother.
EDCS-22100795.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the dedication of a tomb by Lucius Trebonius Spinther for his deceased brother, Lucius Trebonius Donatus.

EDCS-22100799.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Trebonia by her given name and father's name.

EDCS-22100800.translation: Valeria Hospita, freedwoman of Lucius, lies here.
EDCS-22100800.summary: This is a funerary epitaph stating that Valeria Hospita, a freedwoman, is buried here.

EDCS-22100802.translation: For Lucius Varius Gallus. Primus and Faustus, his freedmen.
EDCS-22100802.summary: This is a funerary epitaph indicating that Primus and Faustus, freedmen of Lucius Varius Gallus, set up this monument for him.

EDCS-22100803.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Varia Hilara by her given name, father's name (implied through freedman status), and freedwoman status.

EDCS-22100806.translation: Lucius Veisinnius, son of Lucius, father. Titia, his wife.
EDCS-22100806.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Lucius Veisinnius and his wife Titia.

EDCS-22100809.translation: She made this for Iucundus, her husband.
EDCS-22100809.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording that Verecunda made this monument for her husband, Iucundus.

EDCS-22100813.summary: This is a funerary epitaph stating that Gaius Vettius Saturninus is buried here.

EDCS-22100814.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Vettia by her given name and father's name.

EDCS-22100815.translation: To Vettia Anthia, my sister. Lucius Vettius Severus [dedicated this].
EDCS-22100815.summary: A dedication by Lucius Vettius Severus to his sister Vettia Anthia.

EDCS-22100816.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Vettienus Iustus. Quintus Vettienus [dedicated this].
EDCS-22100816.summary: A funerary dedication by Quintus Vettienus to Quintus Vettienus Iustus.

EDCS-22100820.translation: Vibia Hilara, freedwoman of Gaius, set this up for her mother, the daughter [of Vibia Hilara].
EDCS-22100820.summary: A funerary monument set up by Vibia Hilara for her mother.

EDCS-22100823.translation: To the departed spirits. Victor lived 65 years. Primus and Victorinus [dedicated this] to their well-deserving father.
EDCS-22100823.summary: A funerary monument by Primus and Victorinus for their father Victor, who lived 65 years.

EDCS-22100824.translation: To the departed spirits. To Victor, freedman of Tamudius. Constitutus [dedicated this].
EDCS-22100824.summary: A funerary dedication by Constitutus to his freedman Victor.

EDCS-22100825.translation: To the departed spirits. For Victoria, dearest daughter. Valeria Attica and Valerius Victor [dedicated this]. She lived 15 years, 9 months, 25 days, a virgin.
EDCS-22100825.summary: A funerary monument set up by Valeria Attica and Valerius Victor for their daughter Victoria, who died at age 15.

EDCS-22100829.translation: [To Ursia Fortunata]. Eufronius(?) Felix [dedicated this] to his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-22100829.summary: A funerary dedication by Eufronius(?) Felix to his wife Ursia Fortunata.

EDCS-22100837.translation: To the departed spirits. For Anutia Cepi[---]. [---]sto[---]r [---]atus [---]AF[---].
EDCS-22100837.summary: A fragmentary funerary dedication to Anutia Cepi[---].

EDCS-22100838.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription mentioning possibly multiple individuals.

EDCS-22100858.translation: To the departed spirits. For Aurelius Alexandrus the younger, who lived 28 years, 4 months, and 9 days. [He was] well-deserving. [Someone] dedicated this.
EDCS-22100858.summary: A funerary dedication for Aurelius Alexandrus the younger, who died at 28 years of age.

EDCS-22100859.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Florentius, of laudable memory, an infant who lived two months and six days, his parents set up this monument in his well-deserved old age.
EDCS-22100859.summary: This is a tombstone for a child, Aurelius Florentius, set up by his parents.

EDCS-22100860.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Vaelilius Julianus, also called Aebudius, who lived sixteen years and thirty-four days, his parents made this for him, well-deserving, as he deserved to do for his parents.
EDCS-22100860.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintus Vaelilius Julianus, set up by his parents, reflecting the reciprocal duty between parents and children.

EDCS-22100861.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caesia Benibola, who lived forty-four years and four months, and with her husband for twenty-eight years and four months and twenty-one days, her father with his three sons placed this in well-deserved remembrance, deposited on the Ides of May.
EDCS-22100861.summary: This is a tombstone for Caesia Benibola, set up by her father and brothers, recording her lifespan and her marriage.

EDCS-22100862.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli sacri, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-22100862.translation: Buried. Capelio, on the second day before the Kalends of October, on a Wednesday. He lived four years, three months. To the spirits of the departed.
EDCS-22100862.summary: This is a burial record for Capelio, noting the date of death and lifespan.

EDCS-22100863.inscription_type: carmina, inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-22100863.translation: Here lies Lucia Fonteia Concordia, wife of Sthenius Callicrates Gudentius, placed next to her son Polycronius, chaste, modest, wise, content with one husband, she brought forth grandchildren, by whom she saw sons. She lived seventy years, whom the citizens always called Mother and attended her funeral with candles and cups. Lucia Fonteia Concordia's sons, grandsons, and freedmen set this up. May he have lasting merit. Whoever wishes to live forever. Sthenius Callicras Gudentius, well-deserving, lived seventy-two years, eight months, ten days, with a serene mind and eyes. His sons and grandsons set this up, and attended the funeral with cups and candles.
EDCS-22100863.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucia Fonteia Concordia and her husband Sthenius Callicrates Gudentius, set up by their descendants, celebrating their virtues and long lives.

EDCS-22100864.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Fonteius Gudentius, who lived forty-five years, five months, and twenty-eight days. His wife and sons set this up in well-deserved, most pious remembrance.
EDCS-22100864.summary: This is a tombstone for Fonteius Gudentius, set up by his wife and sons.

EDCS-22100865.translation: To the spirits of the departed. A place for Fonteia Gaudentia, who lived fourteen years, six months, and [number illegible] days.
EDCS-22100865.summary: This is a tombstone or burial record for Fonteia Gaudentia, noting her age at death.

EDCS-22100867.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gellius Victorinus, who lived thirty-three years. Aurelia Sabina for her incomparable husband.
EDCS-22100867.summary: This is a tombstone for Gellius Victorinus, set up by his wife Aurelia Sabina.

EDCS-22100868.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gellius Capitolino, who lived eight years and nine months. Aurelia Sabina, mother, for her most devoted son, well-deserving.
EDCS-22100868.summary: This is a tombstone for Gellius Capitolino, set up by his mother Aurelia Sabina.

EDCS-22100869.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gellia Acinia, dearest mother, deserving of merit for her piety. Antonia Onagris, her daughter, set this up.
EDCS-22100869.summary: This is a tombstone for Gellia Acinia, set up by her daughter Antonia Onagris.

EDCS-22100870.translation: Neranio Feliciano, the Caesars made him, well-deserving.
EDCS-22100870.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Neranio Feliciano, likely by imperial authority given the reference to 'Caesares', and is dated to the imperial period.

EDCS-22100871.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Voelius Sozomenus placed this for his well-deserving wife, Nerania Iuliena, with whom he lived a pleasant life for 30 years.
EDCS-22100871.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Nerania Iuliena, set up by her husband Quintus Voelius Sozomenus, and is dated by the duration of their marriage to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22100872.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Nonius Venustianus, father, well-deserving. His sons and wife placed this for their most loving husband.
EDCS-22100872.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Nonius Venustianus, set up by his children and wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22100873.translation: Deposited on the 10th day before the Kalends of May.
EDCS-22100873.summary: This inscription records a burial date, April 22nd, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22100874.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Trebonius Seleucus, father of laudable memory. His sons and heirs placed this for him, well-deserving.
EDCS-22100874.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Trebonius Seleucus, set up by his sons and heirs, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22100875.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Atilius Iustus placed this for his Ulpia Victoria, most laudable wife.
EDCS-22100875.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ulpia Victoria, set up by her husband Atilius Iustus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22100877.translation: For Lucius Petroni<u_s> Dexter, bishop, father, who lived 66 years, for his dearest father. Lucius Petronius' five sons placed this. Buried the 3rd Ides of December, under the consulship of Provianus and Iulianus.
EDCS-22100877.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Petroni<u_s> Dexter, a bishop and father, erected by his five sons and dated by the consuls Provianus and Iulianus to the 4th century CE.

EDCS-22100878.translation: Of good memory. For Iulia sanctissima, from the family of sancta Asinia Felicitas, who lived 37 years. Pomponius Felicissimus placed this for his incomparable wife. Buried on the 13th day before the Kalends of January, on the day of the Sun.
EDCS-22100878.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia sanctissima, set up by her husband Pomponius Felicissimus, and dated to the Roman Imperial period, possibly with a Christian association due to the mention of 'day of the Sun'.

EDCS-22100880.translation: Aurelius Melitius, a faithful Christian infant foreigner, is placed here, who lived 4 years and 2 days, who died on the night of the Paschal Saturday vigil and was deprived of life, and was buried on the day of the Sun, the 6th day before the Kalends of April. His father placed this for his dearest son.
EDCS-22100880.summary: This is a Christian funerary epitaph for Aurelius Melitius, an infant, and is dated to the Roman Imperial period, specifying the Easter vigil and the burial on the following day, April 2nd.

EDCS-22100881.translation: Of good memory. For Casperia Iulianeta, who lived 40 years. Her father placed this for her, well-deserving.
EDCS-22100881.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Casperia Iulianeta, set up by her father, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22100882.translation: To Gellia Gurianena, Sentius Crescens erected this monument to his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-22100882.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his wife, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-22100883.translation: Memorial to Geminus. Aurelia Timocratia to her incomparable and most rare husband, well-deserving.
EDCS-22100883.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a wife to her husband, highlighting his exceptional qualities, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-22100884.translation: To the departed gods. To Marcus Iuventius Dionysius and his most excellent father. Iustus his son and Montanilla his wife erected this to him as a well-deserving person.
EDCS-22100884.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to a father and son by a son and wife, a common practice in Roman sepulchral monuments.

EDCS-22100886.translation: For good memory. Pisentia Gentiana placed this for her husband Claudius Leontianus, well-deserving, and for her sons, out of duty. Buried on the 14th day before the Kalends of December.
EDCS-22100886.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her husband and sons, including a specific burial date, likely from the 3rd or 4th century CE.

EDCS-22100887.translation: On the 14th day before the Kalends of October. Sallustius Verianus, who lived 20 years, 8 months, and 7 days and 7 hours. Locria Magna placed this for her husband, most sweet.
EDCS-22100887.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age and date of death for Sallustius Verianus, erected by his wife, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-22100888.translation: Sentius Respectus, an exorcist. Minucia Orestina to her wife, who lived 60 years, erected this well-deserving monument.
EDCS-22100888.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sentius Respectus, identified as an exorcist, erected by his wife, suggesting a Christian or syncretic context.

EDCS-22100889.translation: Neofytus, dearest son. Sulpicius Vincentius, who lived 23 years, 6 months, and 6 days. Sulpicii Felissimus and Gelliane, his parents, erected this.
EDCS-22100889.summary: A funerary epitaph for Neofytus, erected by his parents, recording his age at death, typical of Roman family monuments.

EDCS-22100890.translation: Burial of Sulpicius Felicissimus, a deacon, who lived 65 years. His wife and sons erected this well-deserving monument.
EDCS-22100890.summary: A funerary inscription for Sulpicius Felicissimus, identified as a deacon, erected by his family, indicating a Christian burial.

EDCS-22100891.translation: To Vibius Mercurius, who lived 7 years and 7 months. Buried on the 12th day before the Kalends of May. Burial.
EDCS-22100891.summary: A funerary epitaph for a child named Vibius Mercurius, recording his age and date of burial, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22100892.translation: To Ulpia Faustina, a neophyte virgin, who lived 13 years, 2 months, and 25 days. Buried on the 6th day before the Ides of July, in peace.
EDCS-22100892.summary: A funerary inscription for a young Christian convert (neophyte) named Ulpia Faustina, detailing her age and burial date.

EDCS-22100894.translation: On the fourth day before the Ides of October, when Urfus and Polemius were consuls.
EDCS-22100894.summary: This inscription records a date, likely related to a public event or a legal transaction, during the consulship of Urfus and Polemius.

EDCS-22100896.translation: Attia Gentia, with her partner Attius Verissimus, for [him] who deserved well, eighty years old.
EDCS-22100896.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for Attius Verissimus, honoring him for his merit and stating his age.

EDCS-22100897.translation: Here lies the foreigner Ciconias, whose name God knows.
EDCS-22100897.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for an unknown foreigner named Ciconias, whose name was known only to God.

EDCS-22100902.text_edition: \n Depositio Redemtae Diocletiano Augusto IIII et Maximiano III consulibus XVII Kalendas Februarias\n
EDCS-22100902.translation: Burial of Redemta, on the seventeenth day before the Kalends of February, in the fourth consulship of Diocletian Augustus and the third of Maximian.
EDCS-22100902.summary: This inscription records the burial of Redemta, dating it to the consulship of Diocletian and Maximian.

EDCS-22100903.translation: Burial of Florentia, daughter of Hilarianus, on the fourteenth day before the Kalends of April.
EDCS-22100903.summary: This inscription records the burial of Florentia, identified as the daughter of Hilarianus.

EDCS-22100904.translation: Buried on the sixth day before the Kalends of February.
EDCS-22100904.summary: This inscription records the burial of Tiberianus, with the date provided.

EDCS-22100905.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph stating that Benignus lies here.

EDCS-22100906.translation: To the departed spirits. Aemilianus, on the sixth day before the Ides of January.
EDCS-22100906.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the 'departed spirits' for Aemilianus, with the date of death.

EDCS-22100907.translation: Burial of Iulius Mercurius, on the third day before the Kalends of May.
EDCS-22100907.summary: This inscription records the burial of Iulius Mercurius, with the date of his interment.

EDCS-22100909.translation: Burial of Locries Magnes, on the fifth day before the Ides of February.
EDCS-22100909.summary: This inscription records the burial of Locries Magnes, with the date provided.

EDCS-22100910.translation: The remains of Leontis, on the 14th day before the Kalends of November.
EDCS-22100910.summary: This inscription records a burial date, likely for a person named Leontis, on October 19th.

EDCS-22100911.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records two personal names, Viator and Ani, with the rest of the text lost.

EDCS-22100920.translation: Here rests [Gra]ta(?), life refreshing you, may the gods be with you, because you were always faithful in all things.
EDCS-22100920.summary: This is a funerary epitaph expressing hope for a deceased named Grata(?) and praising her faithfulness.

EDCS-22100924.translation: To the departed spirits. For Gaius Umbricius Celer, son of Lucius, of the Pomptina tribe, from Arretium, an equestrian officer of the 9th praetorian cohort, stationed in the centuria of Cominius; he served for 16 years and lived for 40 years. Lucius Umbricius Clemens, primus pilus, erected [this] on his own land, deserving. To the departed spirits. For Gaius Umbricius Celer from Arretium, equestrian officer of [...]
EDCS-22100924.summary: This is a detailed funerary inscription for Gaius Umbricius Celer, a praetorian officer, including his service and life span, erected by his relative Lucius Umbricius Clemens.

EDCS-22100925.translation: Sacred to the Nymphs. Lucius Trebonius Fortunatus, freedman of Paternus, set this up in fulfillment of a vow, and he arranged for the statue, base, and shrine to be made.
EDCS-22100925.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Nymphs by Lucius Trebonius Fortunatus, who commissioned a statue, base, and shrine.

EDCS-22100926.translation: Lucius Granius Pudens, a veteran of the 7th praetorian cohort, gives 8,000 denarii as a gift, so that his descendants may receive the interest in use and that on his birthday, consecrated oil shall be provided in a lamp, which he has endowed from the interest of his own funds to the Unconquered God Mithras.
EDCS-22100926.summary: This is a foundation inscription by Lucius Granius Pudens, a veteran, establishing a perpetual endowment for the cult of Mithras, to provide oil for a lamp and for his descendants to receive interest.

EDCS-22100927.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Valerius Justus, who lived 14 years, 11 months, 14 days. Valerius Eumofus and Comimia Iusta [erected this].
EDCS-22100927.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Valerius Justus, who died at a young age, and is presumably dedicated by his parents or relatives.

EDCS-22100928.translation: To the departed spirits. For Quintus Caecilius Euhelpistus Avitianus, who lived 32 years, 1 month, 8 days. Caecilia Helpidos, his most unhappy mother, made [this].
EDCS-22100928.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Quintus Caecilius Euhelpistus Avitianus at age 32, dedicated by his grieving mother, Caecilia Helpidos.

EDCS-22100930.translation: To Jupiter, Best, Greatest, Supreme, Most Exalted. Titus Sextius Verianus, for the health of his son Cornelianus, a most distinguished man, of consular rank, [...]
EDCS-22100930.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus made by Titus Sextius Verianus for the well-being of his son Cornelianus, a man of consular rank.

EDCS-22100933.translation: For Gaius Vettius, son of Publius, of the Falerna tribe, as aedile, by his will. Vettia, daughter of Gaius, daughter of Publius, his wife; Publius Vettius, son of Gaius, son of Gaius; Gaius Vettius, son of Gaius [...]
EDCS-22100933.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by will for Gaius Vettius, son of Publius, who held the office of aedile, with a list of family members.

EDCS-22100935.text_edition: \n Aulus Ferrarius Auli libertus Salvius fecit sibi et suis et Vibiae <Cn=OT>(aei) filiae Gallae Vettiae Luci libertae Niceni Aulo Messio Auli liberto Diogeni medico \n
EDCS-22100935.translation: Aulus Ferrarius Salvius, freedman of Aulus, made this for himself, his family, and for Vibius Gallus, daughter of Vettius Lucius, freedwoman Nicene, and for Aulus Messius Diogenes, physician and freedman of Aulus.
EDCS-22100935.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Aulus Ferrarius Salvius for himself and his family, including several freedpersons and a physician.

EDCS-22100936.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Valerius Maximus served in the Praetorian cohort at Ravenna for 22 years and lived for 60 years. Helvius Priscus made this for the deserving.
EDCS-22100936.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Valerius Maximus, a soldier in the Praetorian Guard at Ravenna, commemorating his military service and lifespan.

EDCS-22100937.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription for Publius Acilius Attianus.

EDCS-22100938.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Vitalis lived 28 years.
EDCS-22100938.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription for Vitalis, who died at the age of 28.

EDCS-22100939.translation: Aprilis, Fortunatus, and Victor, his sons, made this for their well-deserving wife.
EDCS-22100939.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by three sons for their well-deserving mother.

EDCS-22100940.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cornel [---] lived 4 months. Tyce, his most devoted nurse, made this for him as he deserved.
EDCS-22100940.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child named Cornel who died at four months old, set up by his devoted nurse.

EDCS-22100941.translation: To Felicitas, dearest daughter. Polyxena, mother, lived 7 years and 28 days.
EDCS-22100941.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a mother for her daughter, Felicitas, who died at the age of 7.

EDCS-22100942.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Filocyrius, dearest son, who lived 2 years, 10 days, and 2 hours. His devoted mother Nice made this for him, not undeserving.
EDCS-22100942.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young child named Filocyrius, who died at the age of 2, set up by his mother.

EDCS-22100943.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Victoria, who lived eighty years, four months, and two months. Relatives and daughter made this for their deserving mother.
EDCS-22100943.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Victoria, who lived to be eighty years old, set up by her relatives and daughter.

EDCS-22100949.translation: Nerusius Vincentius, who lived 27 years. Aufidia Sirica, his wife, made this for her well-deserving goldsmith from Lake Callines.
EDCS-22100949.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Nerusius Vincentius, a goldsmith, set up by his wife.

EDCS-22100952.translation: To Aulus Pocanus Severus, who lived 45 years. Lucius Pocas Probus made this.
EDCS-22100952.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aulus Pocanus Severus, erected by Lucius Pocas Probus.

EDCS-22100953.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Sextius Ivaris, patron, made this for Sextia Prisca.
EDCS-22100953.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sextia Prisca, erected by her patron Sextius Ivaris.

EDCS-22100954.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Publius Visulvius Secundus, who lived 38 years, 10 months, and 25 days. His most unhappy mother, Mete[3]ia Victoria, and his most unhappy sister, Visulvia Dextra, made this.
EDCS-22100954.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Visulvius Secundus, erected by his mother and sister.

EDCS-22100956.summary: A simple farewell inscription for Stratonica.

EDCS-22100961.translation: Publius Valerius Protus, freedman of Sextus, and Valeria Peristera, freedwoman of Publius, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-22100961.summary: An inscription indicating that Publius Valerius Protus and Valeria Peristera commissioned a work.

EDCS-22100962.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Augustus. Plotus Statius, son of Publius, the Augustalis, dedicated this.
EDCS-22100962.summary: A dedication to Emperor Augustus by Plotus Statius, an Augustalis.

EDCS-22100963.translation: To Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix, greatest conqueror of Parthia and Britain, and to Decimus Caelius Balbinus, consul, dedicated on the 5th day before the Nones of May. Executed by Porcius Severianus, curator of the public affairs of the people of Cosanum, through the legate Ennius Hyacinthus. To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix, greatest conqueror of Parthia and Britain, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 16th time, imperator for the 2nd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the country, the people of Cosanum [dedicate this] to his tireless indulgence.
EDCS-22100963.summary: A dedication by the people of Cosanum to Emperors Marcus Aurelius Antoninus and Decimus Caelius Balbinus, likely commemorating imperial visits or beneficence, with administrative officials named.

EDCS-22100964.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Antoninus Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 4th time, consul for the 2nd time, father of the country. The people of Cosanum, devoted to his divine will and majesty, [dedicate this] by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-22100964.summary: A dedication by the people of Cosanum to Emperor Gordian III, passed by a decree of the town council.

EDCS-22100965.translation: [To the unconquered, pious, and fortunate Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power, Gothicus Germanicus...]
EDCS-22100965.summary: A fragmentary inscription dedicated to an emperor, likely Aurelian, noting his titles and victories.

EDCS-22100966.translation: To Emperor Caesar Lucius Domitius Aurelianus...
EDCS-22100966.summary: A fragmentary inscription beginning the dedication to Emperor Aurelian.

EDCS-22100968.translation: Lucius Domitius Papus, freedman of Ahenobarbus; Domitia, freedwoman of Papus, Arche.
EDCS-22100968.summary: This inscription lists the names of two freedpeople, Lucius Domitius Papus and Domitia, likely marking a tomb or grave.

EDCS-22100969.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcellina, who lived for 20 years, made this for Nigrio, her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-22100969.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Marcellina for her husband Nigrio, who died after 20 years of marriage.

EDCS-22100979.translation: With Gaius Ritina Iunianus and Lucius Calvisius Vonnenus acting as curators. To Gaius Didius Silvester, son of Gaius, of the Sabatina tribe, twice-elected official for justice, quaestor of public money and funds for the poor; the six men of the Augustales and the urban populace erected this monument for him, his most worthy Saturninus, because of his merits. On the occasion of his dedication, he distributed a denarius each to the people and gave them a dowry of 800 sesterces, from the interest of which, on the 23rd of February, his birthday, the Augustales and the urban populace, with much attendance and distribution of gifts, may perform their duties. The site was granted by decree of the decurions. With Gaius Pomponius Achilles (?) and Lucius Petisius Octavianus, Quintus Haterius Clemens, [---] Maximus and Urbanus as consuls (?), overseeing the work.
EDCS-22100979.summary: This inscription records the honors bestowed by the Augustales and urban populace of a town (likely Volsinii) upon Gaius Didius Silvester, an official, for his merits, including a distribution of money and a dowry, and marks the granting of a burial site by the decurions.

EDCS-22101023.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Germanicus Sarmaticus, son of the deified Antoninus Pius, brother of the deified Verus Parthicus Maximus, grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 20th time, acclaimed emperor for the 8th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country.
EDCS-22101023.summary: This is an inscription on a monument or statue honoring Emperor Marcus Aurelius, detailing his imperial titles and lineage up to Nerva, dating to his 20th year of tribunician power and 8th acclamation as emperor.

EDCS-22101026.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Britannicus Maximus, son of the deified Septimius Severus Pius Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 14th time, acclaimed emperor for the 2nd time, consul for the 3rd time, proconsul, father of the country. The Senate and people of Volsinii [honored] Julia Augusta, mother of the emperors and camps, and the senate [---].
EDCS-22101026.summary: This dedication from the people of Volsinii honors Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius (Caracalla), detailing his extensive imperial titles and ancestry, and also likely honors Julia Domna, mother of the emperors.

EDCS-22101028.translation: [---]ius, son of Aulus, of the Pomptina tribe, Pal[---], quaestor, tribune of the plebs [---], [---] III
EDCS-22101028.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription that lists senatorial or political offices held by an individual, including quaestor and tribune of the plebs.

EDCS-22101030.translation: [---] candidate for tribune of the plebs(?), quaestor, patron of the people of Italy in Volsinii, his homeland, and also of the people of Ferentium and Tibur, and of the colony of Italicenses in the province of Baetica; praetor of Etruria and fifteen peoples; priest of the Caeninenses. Marcus Helvius Clemens, son of Marcus, from Arnums, originating from Carthage, prefect of horsemen of the first wing of the Canninefate cavalry; his most sacred and rare governor. With Lucius Aconius Callistus, tribune of the soldiers of the 14th Legion Gemina Severiana, overseeing the work.
EDCS-22101030.summary: This inscription details the career of Marcus Helvius Clemens, a Roman official and military commander, listing his various political, religious, and military posts, and names his patron and the supervising officer.

EDCS-22101031.translation: To Lucius Aconius Callistus, of most distinguished memory, who lived 72 years, 9 months, and 20 days. Oratia Marina, his dearest wife, [erected this]. Cynegi.
EDCS-22101031.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Lucius Aconius Callistus, who lived to be 72 years old, erected by his wife Oratia Marina.

EDCS-22200480.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Graecinius Iustus, evocatus of the Emperor, veteran.
EDCS-22200480.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Caius Graecinius Iustus, a veteran soldier who served as an evocatus (a veteran recalled to service).

EDCS-22200481.translation: In the consulship of Appius Claudius Iulianus II and Lucius Bruttus Crispinus, on the 23rd of January. In the meeting hall of the guild of craftsmen of the citizen body of Volsinii, which Titus Sossius Hilarus and Caetennius Onesimus, quinquennales, organized, and where the same quinquennales made speeches, [they resolved] how much love and affection Laberius Gallus, a centurion of the first rank and an outstanding man, has shown in his initiatives towards our guild, [and] how his benefits, conferred upon us for a long time, are confirmed. And therefore, we elect Ancharia Luperca, his wife, daughter of the late Ancharius Celer, a man of good memory, whose descendants and lineage have served our homeland with sincere loyalty, as patroness of our guild in their honor and for the chastity of her character and the sanctity of ancient custom. We shall also place a bronze statue of her in our meeting hall, next to her husband, the same Laberius Gallus. What it pleased [the assembly] to do regarding her, the quinquennales have unanimously decreed as just and deserved. So that Ancharia Luperca, an honorable matron of holy disposition and endowed with ritual discipline, in honor of Laberius Gallus, an outstanding man and centurion of the first rank, her husband and patron of our guild, and in memory of her father, the late Ancharius Celer, a most worthy patroness, we elect her. And we shall place a bronze statue of her in our meeting hall next to her husband, the same Laberius Gallus, so that her piety towards us and our regard for her may be publicly visible. We shall also affix a tablet of patronage in her house.
EDCS-22200481.summary: This inscription records the decision of the guild of craftsmen of Volsinii to elect Ancharia Luperca as patroness and erect a bronze statue in her honor, due to the merits of her late husband Laberius Gallus and her own character, under the consulship of Appius Claudius Iulianus II and Lucius Bruttus Crispinus.

EDCS-22200489.translation: Lucius Cominius, son of Lucius, grandson of Aulus, Caius Canuleius, son of Lucius, Titus Tullius Kanus, son of Titus, and Lucius Hirrius Latinus, son of Lucius, served as the four magistrates and oversaw the construction of the theatre and stage at their own expense.
EDCS-22200489.summary: This inscription records the funding and oversight of a theatre and stage by four magistrates in Volsinii, likely during the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-22200495.translation: To the departed spirits. Serenus, belonging to the public administration of the people of Volsinii.
EDCS-22200495.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Serenus, who held a public office in Volsinii, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-22200496.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely refers to an object or structure dedicated to the emperor Nero, dating to his reign (54-68 AD).

EDCS-22200499.translation: To the departed spirits. Of Fortunatus, the sacristan.
EDCS-22200499.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Fortunatus, who served as a sacristan, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-22200500.translation: To the departed spirits. Haevelpisti, to Beneaccipio, from the association of the Dianes from the public housing.
EDCS-22200500.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by the association of the Dianes to Beneaccipio, possibly a member or official, from public housing, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-22200501.translation: To the departed spirits. The collegium of Silvani Gemini made (this) for Julius Hermetis.
EDCS-22200501.summary: This funerary dedication was made by the collegium of Silvani Gemini for Julius Hermetis, likely during the Imperial period.

EDCS-22200502.translation: To the departed spirits. The collegium of companions made (this) for Silvinus.
EDCS-22200502.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by a collegium of companions to Silvinus, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-22200509.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Muttius Onesimus made (this) for Abrochia Ursa, his dearest and well-deserving wife.
EDCS-22200509.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Caius Muttius Onesimus for his wife Abrochia Ursa, likely during the Imperial period.

EDCS-22200513.translation: To the departed spirits. For Quintus Afranius Felix, freedman of Quintus, his most excellent patron. Afrania Artemisia.
EDCS-22200513.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by Afrania Artemisia to her patron Quintus Afranius Felix, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-22200514.translation: To the departed spirits. For Veneria. Afrania Artemisia, freedwoman of Quintus, and Caelius Silvester, her parents, (dedicated this) to their most dutiful daughter, who lived 9 years and 6 months.
EDCS-22200514.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by the parents of the deceased child Veneria, who died at the age of nine, likely during the Imperial period.

EDCS-22200515.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aulus Volcatius Ingenuus, to his well-deserving wife Afrania Veneria.
EDCS-22200515.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his deceased wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22200521.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Silvanus made this for his well-deserving wife Aurelia Vitalis.
EDCS-22200521.summary: This is a funerary epitaph erected by a husband for his wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22200530.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Canuleia Ethis, who lived 10 years. Ivius Iustus...
EDCS-22200530.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Canuleia Ethis, who died at the age of 10, with the epitaph likely completed by Ivius Iustus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22200534.translation: To the spirits of the departed. The heirs made this for Clodius Celer.
EDCS-22200534.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by the heirs of Clodius Celer, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22200535.translation: To the spirits. Romanus, freedman, for Sextus Cominius Corneli Crispus.
EDCS-22200535.summary: This is a funerary inscription by a freedman for his patron, Sextus Cominius Corneli Crispus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.
